templates.expositionText: I've been chasing them off with a pitchfork for a week and I can't keep doing it. {enemy_type_plural} have been getting bolder every night, and I'm running out of nerve. I need {kill_count} of them put down before they figure out I'm bluffing.
templates.acceptText: Oh, thank you. I was starting to think I'd have to do something stupid myself.
templates.declineText: No, I understand. I'll manage. I always manage, somehow. That's the part that scares me.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I slept last night. First time in days. But the trouble isn't just the {enemy_type_plural} themselves. There's the mess they made of everything I'd put aside.
templates.conflict1Text: They tore through my stores and I've got nothing left to work with. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} just to get back on my feet. I hate having to ask for more, but I'm out of options.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's enough to start over with. I can feel my hands not shaking when I count it out. It's been a long time since I could say that.
templates.resolutionText: I'll be all right now. Truly. Take {reward_item}. It's not what your time was worth, but it's what I can give and mean it. You gave me back my own home.

skillCheck.passText: The truth is I haven't told anyone here how bad it got. Two nights ago I sat up with a kitchen knife and cried until the sun came up. I'm not built for this and I'm tired of pretending I am.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a hard stretch. I'd rather not get into the details.

templates.rewardFlavor: what coin I've kept hidden and a meal whenever you pass through

templates.expositionText: I wouldn't be asking if I had anywhere else to turn. I need {gather_count} {quest_item}. My workbench is bare and the order I owe was due last week. If I miss it I lose the trust I spent years building.
templates.acceptText: You don't know what that means. Truly. I was bracing myself to hear no.
templates.declineText: I see. Well. I'll figure something out, I always do, even when I shouldn't have to. Please don't think badly of me for asking.
templates.expositionTurnInText: This is exactly what I needed. I can breathe a little. There's one more thing, though, someone in {target_npc_settlement} owes me a favor I've been too proud to call in.
templates.conflict1Text: Find {target_npc} for me. They're a {target_npc_role} over in {target_npc_settlement}, and they promised they'd help if I ever asked. I never have until now. Tell them it's me, and that I'm not asking for fun.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: They said yes? I half thought they'd forgotten me. That's a kindness I won't waste.
templates.conflict2Text: There's still the matter of the {enemy_type_plural} that started all of this. They've been picking at us like we're already finished. {kill_count} of them dealt with would let me sleep without listening for the door.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Quiet. The first real quiet in weeks. I didn't know how loud the worry was until it stopped.
templates.resolutionText: I have my work back, I have my pride back, and I have a friend in {target_npc_settlement} again. {reward_item}. Take it, please. I'd give more if I had it, and one day I might.

skillCheck.passText: All right. The truth is the order isn't just an order. It's for someone whose family already buried one child this year. I couldn't look them in the eye and explain why their gift wasn't ready. That's the part I haven't been saying out loud.
skillCheck.failText: It's just an order. A late one is bad enough. Let's leave it there.

templates.rewardFlavor: the last of my savings and a standing welcome at my table
templates.targetNpcOpener: They sent you? I've been expecting this for a while, honestly. They helped me through a bad stretch years ago and wouldn't let me repay it, so I promised instead. Tell them the answer is yes, and it's been yes since before they asked.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I should have gone to them first. I could see the trouble building from over here.

templates.expositionText: Please. I don't know who else to ask. The {enemy_type_plural} have been creeping closer to {settlement_name} every night, and I've watched them long enough to know they're not going to stop. I need {kill_count} of them gone before I'm putting down my own neighbors.
templates.acceptText: Bless you for that. I mean it. I was starting to forget what hope sounded like.
templates.declineText: All right. All right. I can't say I blame you, it's not your trouble. But please, if you change your mind, I'll still be here. Probably.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You came back in one piece. That alone is more than I let myself imagine. But I have to be honest with you. Those weren't the only ones.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a second pack working the other side of {settlement_name}. I didn't tell you about them because I thought one ask was already too much. {kill_count} more {enemy_type_plural}, and we might actually make it through the season.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I can't believe I'm standing here saying it's over. Or near to it. Even {settlement_npc} stopped looking over their shoulder this morning.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one last thing, and I promise it's the last. They wrecked our food stores when they were prowling around. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} put aside or we won't see spring. It's not glory work, I know.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That'll get us through. I keep wanting to cry and I keep not letting myself, because I'm afraid if I start I won't stop.
templates.resolutionText: You saved this place. I'm not exaggerating and I'm not being dramatic, I'm just saying the thing as it is. Take {reward_item}. It's most of what I have left, and it's the easiest debt I've ever paid. We'll remember you here.
templates.rewardFlavor: the silver I'd saved for a worse winter and my lasting gratitude

templates.expositionText: My sister's been staying in {target_npc_settlement} since the cold snap and I haven't heard from her in nine days. {settlement_npc} brought back word that she's taken ill, and I can't leave the children to go myself. Please, find {target_npc} there. They're the {target_npc_role} who took her in, and they'll know how she really is.
templates.acceptText: Bless you. Just tell her I'm coming as soon as I can find someone to mind the little ones. Tell her that, please.
templates.declineText: Don't. Don't say no to me. She's the only family I have left and I can't get to her, do you understand? I'm begging you.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You spoke to {target_npc}? And she's holding on? I can breathe a little. But if she's still that weak, there's something I need you to bring her.
templates.conflict1Text: She used to swear by a particular {quest_item} when our mother was sick. It always brought her around. I don't have any here. Bring it back to me and I'll get it to her tonight, I swear I will.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Oh, thank everything. I'll have this in her hands by sundown if I have to walk through snow to do it.
templates.resolutionText: She's going to be all right. {target_npc} sent word this morning and she's already asking when she can come home. Take {reward_item}. It isn't enough, but it's what I have, and {reward_flavor}. You gave me back my sister.

skillCheck.passText: I haven't told anyone this but I keep thinking about our mother. She went the same way. A cough, then a fever, then nothing. I'm not asking you to save my sister from the illness, I'm asking you to save me from losing her the same way I lost everyone else.
skillCheck.failText: She just needs help. That's all there is to it. I can't think past that right now.

templates.rewardFlavor: a debt I'll never forget
templates.targetNpcOpener: Her sister sent you? I'm glad someone finally came. She's been holding on, but the fever keeps circling back, and I've been doing everything I know. She asks about home every morning when she wakes.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell her family she's in good hands. I won't leave her side.

templates.expositionText: My boy went out two days ago and hasn't come back. I'd go after him myself but my leg won't carry me far anymore. {enemy_type_plural} have been thick out there lately, and I need {kill_count} of them cleared before anyone has a chance of finding him alive.
templates.acceptText: Go, please go. And if you see any sign of him out there, don't wait for me, just bring him home.
templates.declineText: He's twelve years old. Twelve. I don't know what else to say to you. I'll keep watching the road myself, then.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a weight off. I can almost picture the path being walkable again. But you'll need something to carry him with if he's hurt. He won't make it back on his own legs.
templates.conflict1Text: Find me a {quest_item} before you go looking. He'll need it more than he'll need words from me, and I want you ready when you find him. Hurry, please. He's been out there too long already.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Good. That's good. I keep telling myself he's the kind of boy who finds shelter, who keeps his head. Please, go now.
templates.resolutionText: He's home. He's home and he's eating like he hasn't eaten in a week, which I suppose he hasn't. I don't have the words for what you've done. Take {reward_item}. It's everything I had set aside for him, and {reward_flavor} besides.

skillCheck.passText: You know how those things hunt. Then you know that he's likely holed up somewhere they can't reach, and that gives me more hope than I've had since he didn't come home. I needed someone to tell me that wasn't a foolish thing to believe.
skillCheck.failText: I don't know much about how they move. I just know my boy is out there with them.

templates.rewardFlavor: a mother's gratitude, whole

templates.expositionText: {settlement_npc} took a bad fall hauling water and they've been getting worse, not better. There's a {quest_item} that would help. I know it would, I've seen it work before, but I haven't been able to leave their side long enough to find one. Please.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'll stay with them until you're back. Just hurry, all right? Hurry.
templates.declineText: You're really going to walk away? Look at me. They could die. I'm not exaggerating to move you, I'm telling you what I'm watching happen.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You found one. Oh, you found one. I'll get this into them right now. But I'm worried about something else, and I have to say it before I lose my nerve.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} that have been circling have us pinned. I can't even get to the next house to ask for help without one of them at the gate. {kill_count} of them cleared and we'd have a way to bring more hands in. I can't keep doing this alone.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I heard the road get quiet. That's a sound I haven't heard in days. I'm not unclenching yet, but I'm closer to it.
templates.conflict2Text: Now I need you to find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role}, and they've helped my family before. They'll know what comes next, and they'll come with you if you tell them it's for me. I need someone who knows what they're doing at the bedside.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} is here. I can hand this off, finally, to someone who actually knows what to do. I think we're going to make it.
templates.resolutionText: {settlement_npc} sat up this morning and asked for soup. Soup. I don't think you understand what that means to me after the week I've had. Take {reward_item}. I'd give you the roof over my head if you asked. You've got {reward_flavor} here. They're going to live.
templates.rewardFlavor: a friend for life
templates.targetNpcOpener: They need me? I should have checked on them sooner. I heard things were rough but I didn't realize how bad it had gotten. I'll pack what I need and come with you.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Don't let them give up before I get there. I've pulled people through worse.

templates.expositionText: The storm took half our roofs and most of what we'd dried for winter. Nobody died, which is the part I keep telling myself. We need {gather_count} {quest_item} to start patching things up before the next bad night, and I've got nobody left to spare from the cleanup.
templates.acceptText: All right. Thank you. I won't pretend that doesn't take a weight off, because it does.
templates.declineText: I understand. We'll do what we can. We always do. It's just going to take longer this way, and longer is the part I'm afraid of.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a real start. We'll have something over our heads tonight that isn't a tarp. But the food stores are the part that's keeping me up.
templates.conflict1Text: We lost most of what was in the larders when the rain got in. We need {gather_count} {quest_item} laid in if anyone's eating come midwinter. It's not glory work, I know. It's just what we need.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's a meal a day for everyone here, give or take. I can look {settlement_npc} in the eye now without flinching. That matters more than I can say.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one last thing. The storm pushed {enemy_type_plural} out of wherever they were, and they've been creeping in to take what we've put aside. {kill_count} of them cleared and we can finish rebuilding without losing it twice.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Quiet for the first time in two weeks. I think we're past the worst of it now. I almost don't trust the feeling.
templates.resolutionText: We're going to come through this. Bruised, lighter than we were, but standing. Take {reward_item}: {reward_flavor}. It's not much, because we don't have much, but every piece of it came from people who know what you did for us. We won't forget the help.

skillCheck.passText: You're right that this isn't the first time. My grandmother used to tell of a season the storms came twice and a settlement two valleys over didn't recover. I'm afraid we're following the same pattern, and I haven't told anyone here that fear because they don't need it on top of everything else.
skillCheck.failText: It's just bad weather. Worse than usual. We'll get through it like we always do.

templates.rewardFlavor: pooled by every household here

templates.expositionText: {enemy_type_plural} came through three nights ago and tore through everything we hadn't already brought inside. We ran them off, eventually, but they're still out there and they're still hungry. I need {kill_count} of them dealt with before they come back to finish what they started.
templates.acceptText: Good. That's good. I haven't had good news in a while, so I'll take it slow.
templates.declineText: Right. Well. We'll keep watch ourselves, then. I'm too tired to argue with you about it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I heard the noise drop. That's the first night I'll sleep without one ear open. But the damage they did is still here, and we have to start somewhere.
templates.conflict1Text: They wrecked our seed stores and we'd already eaten most of the surplus. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} or we won't have anything to put in the ground come spring. That's the part I haven't told the others yet.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's enough to plant with. I can stop dreading the conversation I was going to have to have. Thank you, truly.
templates.resolutionText: We'll plant in spring and we'll eat in summer, and that's all I was asking the world for. Take {reward_item}, {reward_flavor}. It came from people who know exactly what you saved them from, and they wanted you to have something real.

skillCheck.passText: You're seeing it more clearly than I let on. The damage is worse than what I described. I've been talking it down so the younger ones don't panic. Half of what we have left is half-spoiled and I've been picking through it in the mornings before anyone wakes up.
skillCheck.failText: It's bad. I won't pretend otherwise. But we're handling it the way you'd expect.

templates.rewardFlavor: what the settlement could spare

templates.expositionText: The fire took the granary, the smokehouse, and most of what I'd stockpiled for the cold months. We're not finished here, but we're a long way from steady. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} just to start putting things right, and every hand we have is busy keeping the children fed.
templates.acceptText: Good. All right. I needed someone to say yes to something today, and I'm grateful it was you.
templates.declineText: I understand. We'll keep at it. It'll take longer, that's all. Longer is just longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's the first solid thing I've had to work with in days. Now I need someone to carry word. I can't leave here, and the people who used to lend us a hand need to know what happened.
templates.conflict1Text: Find {target_npc} for me. They're a {target_npc_role} in {target_npc_settlement} and we have an old arrangement, the kind nobody writes down. Tell them about the fire and tell them I'm calling in the favor.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} agreed? Of course they did. They were always better to us than they had to be. That'll bring real help in by week's end.
templates.conflict2Text: While we wait, there's still the matter of feeding everyone. I need {gather_count} {quest_item}, enough to stretch us until the help arrives. I hate asking for more, but the children don't care whose fault any of this is.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Everyone ate tonight. Everyone. That hasn't been true since the fire and I almost forgot what it felt like to set the bowls down.
templates.conflict3Text: One more thing, and then I swear I'll stop asking. {enemy_type_plural} have been working at what's left of our supplies, looking for scraps. {kill_count} of them cleared off and we can finally start rebuilding without losing more.
templates.conflict3TurnInText: The yard is quiet. I walked through it just now and didn't hear a single thing moving where it shouldn't be. That's a strange peace, but it's peace.
templates.resolutionText: We're going to be all right. Smaller, lighter, with more empty seats at the table than I'd like, but all right. Take {reward_item}: {reward_flavor}. Every piece of it was given on purpose. You came when you didn't have to. We won't forget that here.
templates.rewardFlavor: coin the households pooled together
templates.targetNpcOpener: A fire, and they're calling in the arrangement? Then things are worse than anyone told me. We made that pact because we both knew a day like this might come. I'll gather what we have and send it.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them I'll have people on the road within the week. That's a promise.

templates.expositionText: My daughter's been running a fever for three days and nothing I have is bringing it down. There's a {quest_item} that would help, but I can't leave her side long enough to find one myself. She cries when I'm not in the room and I can't bear it.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. Truly. Now I don't have to choose between her and the thing she needs.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll go myself, then, when she finally sleeps. I'll only be gone a little while. She probably won't notice. She probably won't.
templates.expositionTurnInText: She let me give it to her without a fuss. I think she could taste that it would help. Her color is already better and I've started to feel like I might survive this.
templates.conflict1Text: There's one more thing, and I'm almost ashamed to ask. {enemy_type_plural} have been working their way closer and the noise terrifies her. Every time they cry out in the dark she clings to me and I can't pry her off. {kill_count} of them gone and she might sleep through a whole night.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: She slept. The whole night. I sat in the chair and watched her chest rise and fall and I cried like an idiot. I don't even know what to say to you.
templates.resolutionText: She's eating again. She asked about the chickens this morning, which is the most ordinary question in the world, and I almost broke down at the breakfast table because of it. {reward_item}. It's hers, really, and she'd want you to have it. Thank you for giving her back to me.

skillCheck.passText: I lost her brother two winters ago to the same kind of fever. I haven't said his name out loud since the day we buried him, and every time she coughs I hear it in my head anyway. I'm not asking you for an herb. I'm asking you to make sure I don't lose another one.
skillCheck.failText: She's just sick. Children get sick. That's all there is to say about it right now.

templates.rewardFlavor: the silver I'd saved for her birthday and a mother's promise

templates.expositionText: My father can't get up the stairs anymore and I've been carrying him myself, which I can do, but not while {enemy_type_plural} are prowling the road I need to walk for everything else we use. I need {kill_count} of them cleared so I can be gone for an hour at a time without praying the whole way.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. Just knowing I can step outside without listening for them is more than I expected today.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll keep doing what I'm doing. He doesn't know I'm scared and I'd like to keep it that way as long as I can.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I walked the long way around this morning and didn't flinch once. He noticed I was gone less time than usual and asked if I'd been visiting. I told him yes. I almost meant it.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a {quest_item} I've been needing for him and I haven't dared go far enough to find one. He won't say it but he's in pain at night and I can hear him through the wall. Bring it to me and he might rest properly for the first time in a month.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: He hasn't called for me once in the last six hours. I almost went in to check he was breathing, just because the quiet felt wrong. He's just sleeping. He's just finally sleeping.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one last thing. Find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role}, and they used to look in on him before the roads got bad. I want them to know he's still here, and I want him to see a friendly face that isn't mine for once.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} will come? Truly? I haven't been able to give him a piece of news that good in months. I'll tell him at supper.
templates.resolutionText: He laughed today. Out loud. I didn't realize how long it had been since I'd heard that sound until it happened and I couldn't move. {reward_item}. Take it, please. It's small, but it's offered by a daughter who has her father back, and that's the largest thing I have to give.

skillCheck.passText: All right. The truth is I haven't slept more than two hours at a stretch since spring. I'm not managing as well as I look like I am, and some mornings I don't know if I have it in me to keep going. I'd never tell him that, but I'm telling you, because I need someone to know.
skillCheck.failText: I'm managing. I always manage. That's what you do for family.

templates.rewardFlavor: what little I've put aside and a place at our table whenever you pass through
templates.targetNpcOpener: He's still going? I've thought about him more than I'll admit. The roads kept me away, but that's a poor excuse and I know it. Tell his daughter I'll come, and I'll bring something warm for the both of them.
templates.targetNpcCloser: She's carrying too much alone. I can see that without being told.

templates.expositionText: My partner came back from the woods last week with an injury that won't close on its own, and I can't leave them long enough to find what I need. There's a {quest_item} that would help me dress the wound properly, and it has to be soon. I'd go myself, but they get worse the moment I'm out of sight.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I mean that with my whole chest. I haven't been able to think past this for days.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll wait until they're sleeping deeply and try myself. I know what could happen if I do. I know.
templates.expositionTurnInText: They held still while I worked on it and didn't cry out once. I think it's already easier for them. But there's something else I should have asked for at the same time, and I didn't because I didn't want to push my luck.
templates.conflict1Text: I need a {quest_item} as well, something to keep them going while their body does the rest of the work. They've barely eaten and they're getting weaker even as the wound improves. Bring me one and I'll do the rest.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: They're sitting up. Sitting up, and asking about the garden, which I think means the worst is behind us. I haven't felt this much hope in days and I'm a little afraid of it.
templates.resolutionText: They walked across the room this morning. Just from the bed to the window, but they did it on their own legs. {reward_item}. Take it, please. We don't have a lot, but what we have feels different now that we both get to keep it.
templates.rewardFlavor: the coin we'd been saving for a better roof and our shared gratitude

templates.expositionText: I'm done waiting them out. {enemy_type_plural} have been picking at us for weeks now. Livestock one night, fences the next, and last night they got bold enough to come right up to the wall. I want {kill_count} of them put down before this turns into something we can't walk back from. It's time someone settled this properly.
templates.acceptText: Good. I was hoping you'd say that. Let's get it handled.
templates.declineText: Then I'll keep waiting for someone with a spine. Don't be surprised when the problem gets worse.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's the kind of work I wanted to see. The pressure on us has eased already. I can feel it. But I don't think we're done yet.
templates.conflict1Text: I want to be sure they don't come back. There's a {quest_item} I need. It'll let me know we've actually pulled the source out, not just trimmed the edges. Bring me that and I'll sleep easy for the first time in weeks.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's it. That's exactly what I needed to see. It's really over now.
templates.resolutionText: I can hear myself think again. Take {reward_item}. It's the least I owe you for finishing what I should have started a month ago. Go on, you've earned it.

skillCheck.passText: You'd know better than me, then. They always come from the same direction, always at the same hour. They're not random. Something's drawing them, and that's what I want broken.
skillCheck.failText: Just trust me when I say it's a pattern. I've been watching long enough to know.

templates.rewardFlavor: the silver I've been setting aside for exactly this and a clean handshake

templates.expositionText: I've had enough of guessing. {target_npc} over in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role}, and they've been keeping notes on the same trouble that's been hounding us. Go talk to them first. I'd rather act on facts than hunches. Once we know what we're dealing with, we end it.
templates.acceptText: That's the right order of things. Find out what they know, then come back to me.
templates.declineText: Suit yourself. Meanwhile the rest of us keep losing sleep, I suppose.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Good. So {target_npc} was right. It's worse than I let myself believe. That settles any doubt I had about doing this.
templates.conflict1Text: Now we put it down. {kill_count} of those {enemy_type_plural} should be enough to break their numbers and make them think twice about coming this way again. I want this finished, not delayed.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's the work, right there. Feels like the air around {settlement_name} got lighter the moment you walked back through.
templates.resolutionText: I won't have to keep one eye on the road from now on, and that's worth more than I can properly say. Take {reward_item}. Spend it however you like. We're square, and the trouble is gone.

skillCheck.passText: Since you're asking the right questions, the timing isn't random. They came right after the last caravan from {other_settlement} stopped running. I think the two are connected, and {target_npc} can fill in the rest.
skillCheck.failText: It's complicated. Easier if you just hear it from {target_npc} directly.

templates.rewardFlavor: good coin and a meal at my table whenever you pass through
templates.targetNpcOpener: You've come from {settlement_name}? Then they've finally noticed what I've been tracking for weeks. The short version is that we're both right to be worried, and the long version would take all night. I've written it all down.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Go back and tell them what I told you. We need to move on this soon.

templates.expositionText: I've been patient long enough. The {enemy_type_plural} have made a habit of testing us, and I want that habit broken. Start by thinning them out. {kill_count} should make them notice. Then we'll trace this back to wherever it actually starts.
templates.acceptText: Now we're getting somewhere. I'll be waiting right here when the first part is done.
templates.declineText: Of course. Walk away. I'll just add this to the list of times I had to do it myself.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Good work. They'll feel that. But knocking them back isn't the same as ending it. I want to know who else has been seeing what we're seeing.
templates.conflict1Text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} has been losing more than we have. They're a {target_npc_role}, and they've been keeping count. Go hear them out, then come back. We'll decide the next step together.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: So {target_npc} has been bleeding even worse than us. That's it then. We're not the only target, and that means the source is bigger than I thought.
templates.conflict2Text: We finish this together. There's a stronger group of {enemy_type_plural} out there, the ones actually driving the rest, and I want {kill_count} of them dealt with. End the pattern, not just the symptoms.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That's done, then. Feels strange to say it out loud. The pattern is broken.
templates.resolutionText: Three weeks ago I'd given up on seeing this resolved in my lifetime. Take {reward_item} and know it's the cleanest thing I've handed anyone in a long time. Whatever happens next, this part is over.

skillCheck.passText: You've got a good eye. I didn't say it before, but I've been finding tracks I don't recognize. Bigger ones, mixed in with the rest. Whatever's leading them isn't the same as what's been hitting us.
skillCheck.failText: It's just the same trouble, week after week. Nothing more to it than that.

templates.rewardFlavor: a proper purse and what's left of my stash of good ale
templates.targetNpcOpener: Someone from {settlement_name}, finally. We've been hit harder than you have, I'd wager. I stopped counting after the second week because the numbers were scaring people more than the actual threat. But I have it all written down.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Whatever you're planning next, count us in. We can't keep absorbing this alone.

templates.expositionText: I've been counting them for nine years. {enemy_type_plural}, the same kind that drove my family off everything we'd built. I'm old now and my hands aren't what they were. I want {kill_count} of them dead by yours. That's where this finally gets to begin.
templates.acceptText: Good. I'd started thinking I'd die with the count still in my head, and that would have been worse than anything they did to us.
templates.declineText: Then walk. I've been waiting nine years already, and I can wait nine more for someone with steadier hands than yours.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's the first wave. The lingering ones, the ones that got comfortable. I felt the count drop in my chest the moment you came back, and it was lighter than I expected.
templates.conflict1Text: The rest are harder. The ones that remember why they came in the first place, the ones who'd recognize my name if anyone said it out loud. I want {kill_count} more {enemy_type_plural} put down. Don't come back until the count is what I've been carrying.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: You finished it. Finished. I haven't said that word about anything in nine years, and I almost don't recognize my own voice saying it.
templates.resolutionText: I thought I'd want to celebrate when this was done. Instead I just want to sit. Take {reward_item}. {reward_flavor}, and no other use for it now.

skillCheck.passText: You recognize the kind of work this is, don't you. Old grudges run in this region. Mine isn't the first and it won't be the last, but it's mine, and I've kept the count cleaner than most ever do.
skillCheck.failText: It's an old story. You don't need the details to do the work.

templates.rewardFlavor: silver set aside nine years

templates.expositionText: They burned the workshop my father left me. Years of his work, gone in a night. There's a {quest_item} I need first. Bring it to me and I'll explain what comes after. I've been waiting a long time to make this right.
templates.acceptText: Then we have an arrangement. I won't forget who answered when no one else would.
templates.declineText: Of course. Why would a stranger care. Move along. I've got a list of people to be disappointed in and you just made it longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You actually brought it. I half thought you wouldn't bother. Good. Now I can finish what I've been planning.
templates.conflict1Text: Now the part that matters. I want {kill_count} of those {enemy_type_plural} put down. The same kind that came that night. I don't care which ones. They all share the same blood as far as I'm concerned.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: It's done. I should feel lighter, and I don't, but I'm not going to pretend that means I was wrong. It just means it cost more than I thought.
templates.resolutionText: I won't be lighting a fire in that workshop again, but at least I can walk past it without flinching. Take {reward_item}. It's {reward_flavor}. I'm closing the books on this, and on a lot of other things while I'm at it.

skillCheck.passText: You see right through me, don't you. The truth is I've been rehearsing this for almost a year. Every morning I wake up and I think about what I'd do if I had the chance. Now I have it, and that scares me almost as much as the night they came.
skillCheck.failText: I've said what I'm going to say. The rest is mine.

templates.rewardFlavor: last of my father's savings

templates.expositionText: I lost my partner to those things last winter. I haven't spoken her name out loud since, and I'm not about to start now. I want {kill_count} of those {enemy_type_plural} dead by your hand. That's where this starts.
templates.acceptText: Then it's settled. I don't want to hear how you feel about it. Just bring me word when it's done.
templates.declineText: Spare me the apologies. You'll find I'm very good at remembering the faces of people who chose to walk away.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Good. Don't tell me what they looked like at the end. I don't want it in my head. There's more to this and I'm going to need you to see it through.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a {quest_item} I need next. She kept things. I want it back in my hands, not for sentiment, for proof that this was real and that I'm not the only one who remembers. Bring it to me.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's hers. I haven't touched anything of hers in months. I almost can't look at it. Almost. Now finish what we started.
templates.conflict2Text: The rest of them. {kill_count} more of those {enemy_type_plural}. I won't be satisfied until their numbers are broken. I know what that makes me. I don't care.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Then it's over. I'm not going to say thank you the way other people would. But I see what you did and I won't forget it.
templates.resolutionText: I expected this to feel like a door closing. Instead it feels like a room that's just gone quiet. Take {reward_item}. {reward_flavor}. Go live a life that doesn't end up like mine, if you can manage it.

skillCheck.passText: All right. Since you're asking, and since you're not going to use it against me, I've already tried to do this myself, twice. Both times I lost my nerve at the last moment. That's the real reason I need you. I can't be the one to do it.
skillCheck.failText: I've told you what I want done. The why is mine to keep.

templates.rewardFlavor: the only thing unburied

templates.expositionText: I've had an idea I can't shake, and I'm finally ready to chase it. There's a {quest_item} I need to get my hands on. It won't be easy, and I'd be a fool to pretend otherwise. But if we can pull this off, I think it changes things for me. For all of us, maybe.
templates.acceptText: Excellent. I knew the right person would come along. I just didn't think it would be today. Let's do this properly.
templates.declineText: Ah, well. The idea isn't going anywhere, and neither am I. I'll keep looking for the right hands.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You actually brought it. I'm holding it and I still half don't believe it. We're past the easy part now, but we're not turning back.
templates.conflict1Text: Here's where it gets thorny. There's {kill_count} {enemy_type_plural} between us and finishing this, and they aren't going to step aside politely. I won't ask you to do anything I wouldn't do myself. But you're better at it than I am.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's it. That's the last thing standing in our way. I can hardly believe we got this far without it falling apart.
templates.resolutionText: We did it. We actually did it. And it's better than I let myself imagine. Take {reward_item}, and know that you didn't just help me, you helped me believe I could finish something this big. {reward_flavor}, truly, for what you did here.

skillCheck.passText: All right, here's the truth. This isn't really about the project. It's about proving to myself that I can still chase a thing and catch it. I haven't felt that way in a long time, and I'm not letting it slip past me.
skillCheck.failText: It's a project. A good one. That's enough reason on its own.

templates.rewardFlavor: my honest gratitude

templates.expositionText: I'm putting together something I've never tried before, and I need help getting it off the ground. First thing. Go talk to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role}, and if anyone has done something like this before, it's them. I want their blessing on the plan before I commit any further.
templates.acceptText: Wonderful. I've been waiting for someone willing to take the first step with me. We'll see this through.
templates.declineText: All right. The idea will keep, even if it stings to set it down for now. I'll find someone else eventually.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You actually got {target_npc} to take it seriously? That's more than I dared hope for. Now I know the plan holds water. Time to commit.
templates.conflict1Text: Now the work begins. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to even start the build. It's a lot, I know. But I've sketched this out a hundred times and I'm not going in half-prepared.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's a real pile. I can almost see the shape of the thing already. We're closer than I thought we'd be at this stage.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one more piece I can't make myself, a {quest_item}, and a tricky one to come by. Bring me that and we cross the line from planning into doing. I'm so close I can taste it.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Look at this. Look at it. We have everything. I haven't been this excited about anything in years.
templates.resolutionText: It's done, and it's better than I imagined. I dared to try and it actually worked. Take {reward_item}, and {reward_flavor} from here on. You weren't a hired hand on this, you were a partner, and I want you to feel like one.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed the drawings, didn't you. Yes. I've been planning this for the better part of a year. I just needed the courage to ask out loud, and that took longer than the planning did.
skillCheck.failText: It's a fresh idea. I've been tossing it around for a while, that's all.

templates.rewardFlavor: an open door, always
templates.targetNpcOpener: Someone's actually attempting it? I tried something similar years ago and I'll tell you straight, the plan matters less than the person behind it. If they're stubborn enough to send you across the region for my opinion, they might actually pull it off.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them I said go. And tell them the first month is the worst part.

templates.expositionText: I'm finally doing it. The thing I've been talking about for two years and never had the guts to start. To get rolling I need {gather_count} {quest_item}. The foundation of the whole undertaking. It's not glamorous, but every great venture starts with the unglamorous part.
templates.acceptText: You won't regret this. I've been waiting for the right person to share the first step with. Now we're moving.
templates.declineText: Suppose I should have expected that. People hear ambitious and they hear trouble. I'll keep at it on my own, then.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's the foundation laid. I feel steadier already, like I'm finally allowed to believe I can do this.
templates.conflict1Text: Here's the part I was dreading. There's {kill_count} {enemy_type_plural} that have been getting in the way of everything I'm trying to do. I need them gone. I can't focus on the work with that hanging over me.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's a weight off. I can actually picture finishing now, instead of just hoping I might. We're past the hardest part, I think.
templates.conflict2Text: Now I need expertise I don't have. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} and they've done something like this before. Go ask them to teach me what they can. I'd rather learn from someone who's lived it than guess my way through.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} actually agreed to share what they know? That's the last piece of doubt gone. Now let's finish this.
templates.conflict3Text: Last thing, I promise. There's a {quest_item} I need to make this real. The centerpiece of the whole thing. Bring me that and I'll have everything I need to finally see this through.
templates.conflict3TurnInText: There it is. The last piece. I'm holding the future of this whole project in my hands and it feels exactly the way I dreamed it would.
templates.resolutionText: I dared to start something I wasn't sure I could finish, and look at it now. Take {reward_item}. And {reward_flavor}, carved into it alongside mine. You've been more than help, you've been the reason I didn't give up halfway. I won't forget that.

skillCheck.passText: All right, since you've been straight with me, I'll be straight with you. I'm scared of this. Every step. I've been pretending I'm not because I knew if I admitted it I'd never start. So thank you for not letting me see how foolish I look.
skillCheck.failText: I've got my plan. I just need the help to make it real.

templates.rewardFlavor: your name on this
templates.targetNpcOpener: They want to learn from me? That's flattering, and a little terrifying, because what I know came from getting it wrong a dozen times first. But I'll share what I can. Better they learn from my mistakes than make new ones.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them I'll put together everything I wish someone had told me at the start.

templates.expositionText: I've been dreaming about this for ages and I'm finally putting it into motion. I want to make something the people here will actually remember, not just useful, but special. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to even start, and I want to start today.
templates.acceptText: Yes! Oh, this is going to be so good. I can already see it coming together in my head.
templates.declineText: Oh. Well, the dream isn't going anywhere. I'll get there eventually, even if it takes me twice as long alone.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Look at all this. This is real now. I'm not just talking about it anymore. We're actually building toward something.
templates.conflict1Text: Now I need the showpiece. There's a {quest_item} that would make the whole thing sing, nothing else will do. If you can find me that, I promise I'll make something worth the trouble.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: It's perfect. It's exactly what I pictured. You've got no idea how long I've been waiting to hold one of these in my own hands.
templates.resolutionText: Look at it. I made this. I actually made this, and it's better than I let myself believe it could be. Take {reward_item}, and take {reward_flavor}, because I wouldn't have anything to give if you hadn't helped me prove what I needed to prove.

skillCheck.passText: All right, I'll tell you. My mother used to say I had a head full of clouds and no hands to match. I've been trying to prove her wrong since the day she said it. This is going to be the thing that finally does it.
skillCheck.failText: It's a dream of mine, that's all. Some dreams are worth chasing even when you can't explain them.

templates.rewardFlavor: the first piece I make

templates.expositionText: I want to be the best at what I do. Not just decent. The best. I need to learn from someone who's already there. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role}, and from what I've heard, they're the one I should be talking to. Go ask them if they'd share what they know.
templates.acceptText: Yes! Thank you for taking me seriously. I know it sounds grand, but I mean it.
templates.declineText: That's a shame. I'll keep working at it the slow way, then. I won't stop, even if it takes years longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} actually agreed to share their knowledge? I could cry. That's exactly what I needed to hear today. Validation that this isn't a foolish dream after all.
templates.conflict1Text: Now I need to put it into practice. {gather_count} {quest_item}. That's what they recommended I start with. Bring me that and I'll make something worthy of what they taught me.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: This is everything I needed. I can already feel my hands itching to get to work. I haven't been this excited in I don't know how long.
templates.resolutionText: I'm not the same person I was a week ago. I know more, I can do more, and for the first time I really believe I'll get where I want to go. Take {reward_item}. It's the start of what I hope becomes a long friendship, and you'll always have {reward_flavor} when you pass through.

skillCheck.passText: You've heard of {target_npc}'s reputation, then? Good. Then you understand why I'm willing to send a stranger across the region just for a conversation. There's no one else within reach who's at that level. Not in {settlement_name}, not in {other_settlement}, not anywhere I know of.
skillCheck.failText: They're someone worth listening to. That's the short of it.

templates.rewardFlavor: a seat at my bench
templates.targetNpcOpener: They want to learn from me? I've had people ask before, and most of them quit after the first real lesson. If they're serious, truly serious, then I'll teach them everything I know. I don't waste time on people who aren't ready to work.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them to start with the basics and not to skip anything. The boring parts are what make the rest possible.

templates.expositionText: I've got a vision and I'm tired of keeping it inside my own head. I want to make {settlement_name} better than it was when I got here, not just patched up, actually better. To begin I need {gather_count} {quest_item}. That's the seed of the whole idea.
templates.acceptText: You don't know what this means to me. I've been waiting for someone willing to start this with me, and here you are.
templates.declineText: Ah. Well, dreams keep, and so do I. I'll find another way. I always do.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Look at that. The first piece of something I've been picturing for months. I can see it now. The rest of it, I mean. It's all going to come together.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a snag, though. {kill_count} {enemy_type_plural} have been making themselves a nuisance and slowing every honest project in the area to a crawl. I need them dealt with so the rest of us can actually get on with creating things.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's a relief I didn't even know I was waiting for. The whole place feels lighter, like the future just opened up a little wider.
templates.conflict2Text: Last thing, and it's the one I'm most excited about. There's a {quest_item} that would be the heart of everything I'm trying to do. It's the one piece I can't substitute. Bring it to me and I can finally say I've done what I set out to do.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: It's exactly what I dreamed of. I'm going to put it right at the center where everyone can see it. People are going to talk about this for a long time.
templates.resolutionText: I look around now and I see something I made happen, not alone, but I started it, and that counts. Take {reward_item}, with my real and lasting thanks. And expect {reward_flavor}, because I get to be proud of myself today, and that's a feeling I want to share.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed I've been at this already, didn't you. Yes. I've been quietly putting pieces in place for weeks. I didn't want to ask for help until I knew the bones of the idea would actually hold up. Now I know they do.
skillCheck.failText: It's just an idea I've been turning over for a while. Nothing fancier than that.

templates.rewardFlavor: a story told for years

templates.expositionText: I'm running low on {quest_item} and I can't make my usual rounds without it. Anything you can scrounge up would save me a lot of trouble. I'd go myself, but my hands aren't what they used to be.
templates.acceptText: Bless you for that. I'll get the kettle on while you're out.
templates.declineText: Ah well. I'll dig around tomorrow and see what I can turn up.
templates.expositionTurnInText: There it is. Exactly what I needed. You've got a good eye for the right thing. There's one more piece to this, though, if you've still got the patience.
templates.conflict1Text: The reason I couldn't fetch it myself is the {enemy_type_plural} that have settled in around here. I don't dare cross the area until someone thins them out. Could you handle {kill_count} of them for me?
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Good. That should make the walk safer for the rest of us, too. I owe you for both pieces of this, not just the one.
templates.resolutionText: That's me set for the season, thanks to you. Take {reward_item}. It's the least I can offer for the time you've spent on this. You've made my work possible again.

skillCheck.passText: Truth is, I've been making do with scraps for nearly a month and pretending I wasn't. Pride's a stubborn thing in folks my age. You're the first person I've actually told.
skillCheck.failText: Oh, it's nothing dire. Just a bit of an inconvenience, you know how it is.

templates.rewardFlavor: what coin I've put aside and a meal whenever you're back this way

templates.expositionText: I've got an order I can't finish without the right pattern, and I never learned it properly myself. There's a {target_npc_role} called {target_npc} over in {target_npc_settlement} who knows the work. Would you mind asking them on my behalf?
templates.acceptText: Appreciated. Tell them I sent you and they'll know who you mean.
templates.declineText: Fair enough. I'll send word the next time a traveler passes through.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} pointed you in the right direction? Good. They've always been straight with me. Now I just need the materials to actually do the work.
templates.conflict1Text: I need {quest_item} to follow the pattern through. It's not exotic, but I haven't had a chance to gather it myself. Bring me what you can find.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Perfect. This is the right grade. I can feel it. There's one more thing pinching me, and then I can finally get to the bench.
templates.conflict2Text: I won't lie, the {enemy_type_plural} prowling near {settlement_name} have me uneasy about leaving the workshop at all. If you could clear out {kill_count} of them, I'd breathe a lot easier while I work.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That's a weight off. I can hear myself think for the first time in days. Come back and let me settle up.
templates.resolutionText: Order's done, on time, and better than I'd have managed alone. {reward_item}. I insist. You earned every bit of it doing what I couldn't.

skillCheck.passText: Why didn't I learn it myself? Because the person who used to teach it stopped coming around years ago, and I never got the nerve to ask anyone else. {target_npc} is the last one I know who still does it the old way.
skillCheck.failText: It's a long story. Just trust me that I need the help.

templates.rewardFlavor: a fair share of what the order pays me, and a proper handshake
templates.targetNpcOpener: They sent you about the pattern? I wondered when someone would come asking. Nobody else does the work this way anymore, and I've been half expecting the knowledge to die with me. I'll write it down for them.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them to take their time with the first attempt. It looks simple on paper but the hands need to learn it separately.

templates.expositionText: I've been wanting to restock properly for weeks now, but the {enemy_type_plural} have made it more bother than it's worth. If you took care of {kill_count} of them, I could actually get on with what I need to do.
templates.acceptText: Right then. I'll have something hot waiting when you're back.
templates.declineText: Suit yourself. I'll keep ducking around them like I have been.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a relief. The whole place feels lighter knowing they're handled. Now for the actual reason I needed them gone in the first place.
templates.conflict1Text: I need {quest_item} to keep things running properly here. It's not glamorous work, but it's what I'd be doing myself if the path weren't a problem. Bring me what you can.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Just what I needed. You've saved me a couple of long days of grumbling. Sit down a minute and I'll fetch your due.
templates.resolutionText: Now I can actually get back to the work I'm supposed to be doing. Take {reward_item} with my thanks. It's good to feel caught up for once.
templates.rewardFlavor: a few coins from the till and a warm cup whenever you're passing through

templates.expositionText: {enemy_type_plural} tore through what we'd set aside for the cold months. Gone, just like that. I want {kill_count} of them put down before they come back to finish the job.
templates.acceptText: Finally. I've been waiting for someone to take this seriously.
templates.declineText: Of course. I'll deal with it, the way I deal with everything else.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a start. I can already feel the knot in my chest loosening. But knocking them down doesn't put back what they took.
templates.conflict1Text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to replace what we lost. It's the same thing they ruined, and I won't feel right until the shelves look the way they should. Help me put it back.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Look at that. Almost like it never happened. Almost. Come inside, I want to settle this properly.
templates.resolutionText: We're whole again. That's what matters. Take {reward_item}. You didn't just fix the problem, you gave me back the feeling that someone in this world cares whether things stay where they belong.

skillCheck.passText: It's not really about the supplies, is it? I built that store with my own two hands over the last three years. Watching it disappear in one night made me feel like I'd never had it at all.
skillCheck.failText: It's a practical loss. Practical losses need practical solutions.

templates.rewardFlavor: what little I have left in coin and a debt I'll remember

templates.expositionText: Something of mine went missing, and I need it back. {quest_item}. It doesn't sound like much, but it's been in my family longer than I have. If you spot one out there, please bring it to me.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been sick over this for days. It means something to have help.
templates.declineText: I see how it is. I'll keep looking on my own, then.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's it. That's actually it. I almost can't believe I'm holding it again. But I need to know how it ended up out there in the first place.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a {target_npc_role} in {target_npc_settlement} called {target_npc} who was around when it disappeared. They might know more than they let on. Would you go ask them what they remember?
templates.conflict1TurnInText: So {target_npc} told you the truth of it. I had a feeling. At least now I know what really happened, and I know what needs doing about it.
templates.conflict2Text: The {enemy_type_plural} that took advantage of all this are still out there. I want {kill_count} of them dealt with so I can stop looking over my shoulder every time I open the door.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Good. That's the last piece. I can finally let myself rest about this.
templates.resolutionText: It's back where it belongs, and I know the full story for the first time. Take {reward_item}. I want you to have it. You gave me back more than just an object.

skillCheck.passText: You recognize the make? Then you know it's older than this settlement. My grandmother carried it when there was nothing here but the road and a promise. Losing it felt like losing her again.
skillCheck.failText: It's old. That's all anyone needs to know.

templates.rewardFlavor: the small purse I keep for emergencies and my honest thanks
templates.targetNpcOpener: I was hoping nobody would come asking about that. I saw more than I said at the time because I didn't want to make trouble. But you're here now, and I'll tell you the truth of it.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Take that back to them. They deserve to know what really happened, even if it stings a little.

templates.expositionText: I lost a full season's worth of {quest_item} to a single bad night. Years of effort, gone. I need {gather_count} of it brought back to me so I can pretend the disaster didn't happen.
templates.acceptText: Then I'm not entirely on my own with this. Good.
templates.declineText: Of course you'd say no. Why would today be any different.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's most of what I'd lost. Looking at it stacked up again, I almost feel like myself. Almost. There's still the matter of what caused all this.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} that did this are still out there acting like they own the place. I want {kill_count} of them put down. Not for revenge. For the simple fact that I shouldn't have to live with the worry.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: There. Now I can sleep without expecting to wake up to another mess. I won't forget this.
templates.resolutionText: I'm whole again. Took longer than I'd have liked, but here we are. Take {reward_item}. You helped me get back what I'd already mourned, and that's worth more than what I'm offering.
templates.rewardFlavor: the silver I'd been saving for a better year and my deepest thanks

templates.expositionText: I've been trying to keep things together for {settlement_name}, and I'm running out of {quest_item}. Bring me {gather_count} and I won't see this through without your help. I won't pretend it isn't a lot to ask.
templates.acceptText: That means more than you know. I haven't had someone in this with me for a long time.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll keep at it the way I have been. The work doesn't stop just because I'm the only one doing it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Look at all that. I haven't seen the supply this full in months. It's a real start, and it's all because you said yes.
templates.conflict1Text: The hardest part is that the {enemy_type_plural} keep undoing what we manage to put together. I need {kill_count} of them dealt with, or none of this matters by the end of the week.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That should buy us some peace. I can already feel the difference in the way folks here are walking around. You gave them that.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one thing I've been needing for ages and could never lay hands on. {quest_item}. With that, I can finish what I set out to do. Could you find one for me?
templates.conflict2TurnInText: You actually found it. I want you to know I'd given up on ever holding one of these again. Come back tonight and let me give you what's owed.
templates.resolutionText: It's done. The thing I've been working toward all this time is finally where it should be. Take {reward_item}. It isn't much, but it's everything I have, and the cause was always more important than what I kept for myself.

skillCheck.passText: I haven't slept properly in months. Not because I'm afraid. Because I keep thinking if I close my eyes too long, the whole effort will unravel. It's a foolish way to live, I know.
skillCheck.failText: I'm doing what needs doing. There's not much more to say about it.

templates.rewardFlavor: what coin I haven't already given to the cause and a place at my table

templates.expositionText: I made a promise to watch over {settlement_name} when no one else would, and I've kept it. But I can't do it alone any longer. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to keep going through the next stretch.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. Truly. You've just shouldered something I've been carrying alone.
templates.declineText: Then I keep going alone. The promise doesn't bend just because help didn't come today.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Every bit of this matters. I can feel the slack going out of the rope. There's still more to handle before I can call it good.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} keep testing us. I need {kill_count} of them dealt with so the rest of what we're doing here isn't undone in a single night.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's the kind of help I prayed for and stopped expecting. The folks here can rest a little easier tonight because of you.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one more thing I need from you, and then I think we're close. I'm short {gather_count} {quest_item}. Bring it back and we can finish this properly.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Good. That's the last of what I needed from out there. There's only one piece left, and it isn't something I can fetch with my own hands.
templates.conflict3Text: I need word sent to a {target_npc_role} called {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They've helped me before and they'll know what to do once they hear how far we've come. Would you carry the message?
templates.conflict3TurnInText: You spoke to {target_npc}? Then it's really happening. I can hardly believe I'm saying that out loud after all this time.
templates.resolutionText: I gave everything I had to this and it was almost not enough. You made the difference. Take {reward_item}. I held it back for the worst day, and instead I get to give it to you on the best one.

skillCheck.passText: You see the way I'm favoring my left side? An old injury I never had time to let heal properly. I'll get to it eventually. Maybe when this is finally done.
skillCheck.failText: I'm fine. Just tired in the way anyone my age would be.

templates.rewardFlavor: the coin I set aside for myself and never spent, and my word as long as I have one
templates.targetNpcOpener: They've gotten this far? I half expected to hear they'd given up by now, and I mean that with respect. What they're doing out there takes more than most people have. Tell me what they need.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them they're not as alone in this as they think. Some of us just took too long to say so.

templates.expositionText: I keep this place running because somebody has to, and lately that somebody is only me. I'm short on {quest_item}, and without it I won't be able to keep my doors open by week's end. Help me find one.
templates.acceptText: I appreciate it more than I can say. Folks here need somewhere to come in out of the dark.
templates.declineText: I'll keep looking on my own. The work doesn't end because one person walked away from it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: There it is. That'll keep me going for a good while yet. There's one more thing I need handled before I can settle into the work properly.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been making the road in unsafe for travelers and locals alike. I need {kill_count} of them put down, not for me, for everyone who counts on this place being a refuge.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's the kind of thing that lets people sleep without their boots on. You've done more than you know.
templates.resolutionText: I can keep doing what I set out to do, and that's because of you. Take {reward_item}. It's what I'd have spent on the work anyway, and I'd rather it go to the person who made the work possible.
templates.rewardFlavor: the modest purse I keep for hard days and a bed here whenever you need one

templates.expositionText: Something keeps getting into my stores at night and it's not adding up. Nothing's broken, nothing forced, just a little less every morning than there was the day before. {target_npc} over in {target_npc_settlement} dealt with something like this a few seasons back, or so I've heard. Could you go ask them what they figured out?
templates.acceptText: Good. I've been turning this over for a week and getting nowhere, and it's nice to have someone else thinking about it.
templates.declineText: Fair enough. I'll keep puzzling at it on my own time, I suppose.
templates.expositionTurnInText: So {target_npc} thinks they know what we're dealing with? That actually fits with what I've been seeing. I had a theory but I wasn't sure I could trust it.
templates.conflict1Text: If {target_npc} is right, there's a way to be certain. Bring me a {quest_item} and I'll set it out tonight and see what happens. Either I'll feel like a fool in the morning or I'll finally know what's been making off with my supplies.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: The {quest_item} did exactly what I hoped it would. I sat up half the night and watched them slip in like they owned the place. Mystery solved. Now I just have a problem to handle.
templates.conflict2Text: Now that I know what they are, I'd rather not share my stores with them anymore. Could you handle {kill_count} of the {enemy_type_plural}? They won't be hard to find now that we know what we're looking for.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That's the lot of them, I'd wager. The stores were untouched this morning for the first time in weeks, and I can finally stop counting sacks before bed.
templates.resolutionText: I'm going to sleep properly tonight, and I have you to thank for it. Take {reward_item}. It's not much, but it's the difference between knowing and wondering, and I owe you for that.

skillCheck.passText: There's one detail I left out because it sounded silly. The losses only happen on nights when the wind comes from a particular direction, and I think whatever it is can smell the grain from a long way off. That's the part that tipped me off this isn't just bad counting.
skillCheck.failText: It's probably nothing dramatic. I just hate not knowing.

templates.rewardFlavor: what coin I can spare and a sack of the grain you helped me save
templates.targetNpcOpener: Stores going missing with nothing broken? I had the exact same problem two seasons ago. It drove me half mad until I figured out the trick. You set out bait that looks like a mistake, not a trap, and you watch.
templates.targetNpcCloser: They'll figure it out once they see it for themselves. That's always the hardest part, believing what's right in front of you.

templates.expositionText: There's a patch of ground near {settlement_name} where nothing has grown right for two seasons now. Same soil as everywhere else, same water, same sun, just dead. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} keeps a closer eye on this kind of thing than anyone I know, and I'd love to hear what they make of it.
templates.acceptText: Good. I was starting to think I was the only one who cared about a piece of dirt, and it's a relief to know I'm not.
templates.declineText: No matter. I'll keep wondering on my own and probably die wondering. Dirt is patient.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} said that? Huh. I would never have put those two things together, but now that you say it out loud, I can see exactly how it might fit.
templates.conflict1Text: If {target_npc}'s thinking lines up with mine, there's a way to test it. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} so I can try the idea out properly. Whatever's been killing the patch, this should either prove or kill the theory before nightfall.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's plenty. I'll set it up myself. By the time the sun's down I'll know whether I owe {target_npc} an apology or a thank you, and probably both.
templates.resolutionText: It worked. Two seasons of staring at dead ground and the answer was so simple I'm a little embarrassed. Take {reward_item}. You saved me from staring at it for a third season, and that's worth more than I'm letting on.

skillCheck.passText: I'll tell you what nobody else around here would believe. The animals avoid that patch too, even the bold ones. Whatever's wrong with the ground, they figured it out before I did, and that's the part that's been keeping me up.
skillCheck.failText: I just want to understand it. That's all.

templates.rewardFlavor: a share of whatever I manage to grow once the patch is fixed
templates.targetNpcOpener: Dead ground near {settlement_name}? I've been wondering about that. Something underneath has been drawing the life out of the soil, and the pattern lines up with the old drainage channels from before anyone was living there.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Have them test it the way I described. If I'm right, they'll see results within the week.

templates.expositionText: I keep finding the same kind of mark scratched into things around {settlement_name}. My fence, the side of the well, the post by my door. It's not vandalism, it's too careful for that. Bring me a {quest_item} so I can compare what's been left here against the real thing, and then maybe I can stop guessing.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been going in circles with this one and a fresh pair of hands will help me finally pin it down.
templates.declineText: Suit yourself. I'll keep collecting little oddities and adding them to my pile of unanswered questions.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's it. That's exactly the shape. I knew I'd seen it before but I couldn't place where, and now I know what made the marks. I don't love what I know.
templates.conflict1Text: {enemy_type_plural} leave that mark when they're scouting a place. They've been studying us, and they'll be back in numbers if we don't make ourselves unpleasant company. I'd feel a lot better if {kill_count} of them never made the report. Can you handle that?
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That should be enough to make them think twice. The marks won't matter once the scouts stop coming home, and I can finally stop staring at my own fence post like it owes me an explanation.
templates.resolutionText: I'm putting this one to bed. The mystery had teeth, but you took them out before they bit anyone. Take {reward_item} with my thanks. I'd rather know what was coming and stop it than wake up to find out the hard way.
templates.rewardFlavor: the small store of coin I keep for trouble, since this counted

templates.expositionText: My brother and I haven't spoken properly in months, all over a tool he says I borrowed and never returned. I didn't, but the move to {target_npc_settlement} has made him remember things wrong. Could you go talk to {target_npc} there? They're his closest neighbor now and they'll know what version of the story he's telling.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I want to fix this, I just don't know how to do it without one of us having to admit we're wrong first, and neither of us is ready for that.
templates.declineText: So I'm on my own with him, then. I should have known better than to expect anyone else to wade into family business.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} said that? So he's been telling people I broke it on purpose. That's worse than I thought, and somehow easier, because at least now I know what I'm actually arguing against.
templates.conflict1Text: If he thinks I broke his tool, the only way to settle this is to bring him the {quest_item} and let him see it for himself. I don't even know if he kept the original. Bring me one and I'll figure out what to do with it.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Holding this in my hands, I feel ridiculous. We've been writing each other off for months over something I could have replaced in an afternoon, if either of us had the sense to ask.
templates.conflict2Text: I want to do this properly. Could you bring me {gather_count} {quest_item} as well? I'll bundle it all together. The replacement, the makings of a meal, an offering. If I show up empty-handed he'll just slam the door, but if I show up with too much, even he won't be able to say no.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That should be enough to get me through his door. I'm nervous, which is stupid. He's my brother. But I'm also feeling something that might be hope, which is less stupid.
templates.resolutionText: We talked. Properly, for the first time since the harvest. Neither of us was right and neither of us was wrong, and we both knew it the moment we sat down. Take {reward_item}. You got me to stop being a coward about my own family.

skillCheck.passText: The truth is I don't even care about the tool. I miss him. We used to work side by side every day and now I see him twice a year if I'm lucky, and I let a stupid argument become an excuse not to admit how much that hurts.
skillCheck.failText: He's stubborn and I'm stubborn. That's all there is to say about it.

templates.rewardFlavor: a meal at my table and the return of a brother I'd half given up on
templates.targetNpcOpener: I know exactly what he's been telling people. He says the tool was broken on purpose. I don't think he even believes it himself anymore, but the story has taken on a life of its own and he's too proud to walk it back.
templates.targetNpcCloser: The two of them need to sit in a room and talk. This won't fix itself through messengers.

templates.expositionText: There's a partner I used to work with, and we had a falling out so bitter that we swore we'd never share a road again. The trouble is the road is the same one we both still need. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} knows us both. Would you start there and see whether they think there's any way back from this?
templates.acceptText: I appreciate you not asking why. The whole story would take all night and most of it doesn't make me look good.
templates.declineText: I see. I suppose we'll keep avoiding each other and pretending the road isn't long enough for us both. It's a poor way to live.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} says he's been struggling? That's not what I expected to hear, and it changes things. I was ready to keep being angry forever, and now I find out he's been quietly losing ground while I sulked.
templates.conflict1Text: {target_npc} mentioned that {enemy_type_plural} have been making the area between us dangerous for both of us. If I go to him and ask his help with it, he'll think it's a trap. But if you handle {kill_count} of them first, I can show up with proof that I cleared the way to see him.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: It's safer than it's been all season. I can't believe it took {enemy_type_plural} to make me consider walking that road again. I'm starting to wonder if I should have gone to him a long time ago.
templates.conflict2Text: I want to bring him something useful, not just words. Could you gather {gather_count} {quest_item}? It's the kind of thing he always needed and could never get enough of. If I show up with that on top of clearing the way, he'll know I mean it.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That's perfect. I'm going to pack tonight and walk over in the morning. Whatever happens between us, I'll know I tried, and that's more than I could have said yesterday.
templates.resolutionText: We didn't fix everything. We're not going back to the way it was, and I think we both knew that the moment we sat down. But we shook hands, and we'll share the road, and that's enough. Take {reward_item}. You gave me back something I didn't think I could get back.

skillCheck.passText: It's not the first time I've let pride do this to me. There was an old friend, years ago, and I let her walk away the same way I've been letting him walk away. I never made it right with her and she's gone now, and I can't make that mistake twice in one lifetime.
skillCheck.failText: It's complicated. I'd rather not get into it.

templates.rewardFlavor: what little coin I have and a story I'll tell properly someday
templates.targetNpcOpener: You're asking if there's a way back? I've been wondering when one of them would break first. They're both miserable, and neither will admit it. He's been struggling, if that matters, and he's too proud to say it where anyone might hear.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them the road between them isn't as closed as they think. But someone has to walk it first.

templates.expositionText: {settlement_npc} and I have been at each other's throats over an heirloom that belonged to our grandmother, and the fight has gotten louder than the heirloom is worth. The only way I can think to settle it is to find a {quest_item} like the one we're fighting over. If I can show {settlement_npc} that the thing itself isn't rare, maybe we can stop pretending this is about the object.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I know it sounds petty from the outside, and from the inside it sort of is too, but I don't know how else to start undoing this.
templates.declineText: So we keep yelling at each other across the well, then. {settlement_npc} will be delighted. I won't.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Holding this in my hand, I feel like an idiot. We've been at each other for weeks over something I could replace in an afternoon. The fight was never about the heirloom, was it.
templates.conflict1Text: Now I have to go talk to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They knew our grandmother better than either of us did, and they'll remember who she actually meant to leave it to. I want to walk into the next argument with the truth instead of just my version of it.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} told me what I needed to hear and what I didn't want to hear, in equal measure. It turns out neither of us was right about grandmother's wishes, which is humbling and also a relief. We can't both lose if neither of us was supposed to win.
templates.resolutionText: We sat down together. We didn't hug, we didn't cry, but we agreed to share the heirloom and the responsibility for it, and that's the closest thing to peace we've had in a year. Take {reward_item}. You stopped two grown people from making fools of themselves over a keepsake.
templates.rewardFlavor: a small token from the heirloom set, since I have one to spare now
