templates.expositionText: I've been mapping the safe paths between {settlement_name} and {other_settlement} for travelers who can't afford a guide. I leave markers and keep them current. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who does the same on their end. I need you to coordinate our routes so the paths actually connect.
templates.acceptText: Good. The paths only matter if they go somewhere. Two half-maps don't help anyone.
templates.declineText: I'll send a letter when I can. The routes drift when nobody tends them.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} was able to match their markers with mine? Excellent. The paths will connect properly now. There's one more thing I need to finish the current stretch.
templates.conflict1Text: I need a {quest_item} for the waypoint stations. The old markers are fading and travelers have been getting lost where they shouldn't. A fresh set will keep people on the right track.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: The markers are placed and the path reads clearly. Now there's just the matter of keeping it safe.
templates.conflict2Text: {enemy_type_plural} have been frequenting the middle section of the route. {kill_count} of them dealt with and the path stays open for everyone who needs it.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: The full route is clear, marked, and safe. Anyone can walk it now without a coin to their name and arrive where they meant to.
templates.resolutionText: The path connects two places that needed connecting, and it costs nothing to use. That's the point of the whole exercise. Take {reward_item}. I keep doing this because I believe everyone deserves a safe road, and today you helped me prove it.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed the glyphs on the marker posts, didn't you. They're not decoration. An old friend taught me a simple warding pattern that discourages predators from crossing the path. I don't fully understand why it works, but it does, and I carve it into every post I place.
skillCheck.failText: It's just a path. I mark it and I keep it clear.

templates.rewardFlavor: a traveler's wage and the satisfaction of a road well kept
templates.targetNpcOpener: You're from the person who marks the western routes? I've been hoping they'd reach out. My paths end at the ridge and theirs start somewhere past it. If we line them up, there's a continuous safe route from here to {settlement_name}.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I'll adjust my markers to meet theirs. Tell them to check the ridge crossing next time they're up there.

templates.expositionText: My grandfather can't feed himself properly anymore and I've been doing the cooking, the cleaning, and the mending while working my own trade. I need a {quest_item} that would make the daily routine easier. Something to lighten the load before it breaks me.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'd never say this to him, but some mornings I sit outside before going in and just breathe for a while. That's the hardest part of my day and it happens before anything starts.
templates.declineText: I'll manage. He managed for me when I was small. I owe him at least as much.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's going to help. The morning routine just got a little shorter, which means I might actually eat breakfast myself for once. But there's another problem I've been ignoring.
templates.conflict1Text: {enemy_type_plural} have been getting closer to the house. He can't run and I can't carry him. {kill_count} of them cleared and I'll stop picturing the worst every time I hear something at the window.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Quiet now. He fell asleep in his chair this afternoon without me having to check the doors first. I didn't realize how much tension I'd been holding until it left.
templates.resolutionText: He patted my hand this morning and said I looked rested. First time in months that's been true. Take {reward_item}. You gave me back the part of the day that was disappearing, the part where I get to be a person instead of just a caretaker.

skillCheck.passText: You led me right into that, didn't you. All right. I resent him sometimes. There, I said it.
skillCheck.failText: He needs me. That's the beginning and end of it.

templates.rewardFlavor: a small purse I've been adding to when I can and my honest thanks

templates.expositionText: My sister is pregnant and her husband is away in {other_settlement} with no word of when he's returning. The {enemy_type_plural} near our homestead have been a constant worry, and she can't sleep for the noise of them. I need {kill_count} of them gone so she can rest.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. She pretends she's fine, but I hear her pacing at three in the morning and I know she's not.
templates.declineText: I'll sit up with her, then. Another sleepless night won't kill me. Probably.
templates.expositionTurnInText: She slept last night. Properly slept, for the first time in I don't know how long. But there's something else she needs and she's too stubborn to say it.
templates.conflict1Text: I need a {quest_item} for her. She's been making do without because she doesn't want to be a burden, but I can see the difference it would make. She won't ask, so I'm asking.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: She tried to refuse it and then started crying. The good kind, I think. The kind that means someone finally noticed.
templates.conflict2Text: Her old midwife {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. I want {target_npc_role} to know the time is getting close. She trusts {target_npc} more than anyone, and having them near would settle her in a way I can't.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} is coming? She'll be so relieved. I've been trying to fill a role I'm not equipped for, and now I don't have to.
templates.resolutionText: She's calm for the first time since her husband left, truly calm. {target_npc} is nearby, the house is quiet, and she told me I could stop hovering. Take {reward_item}. You helped me take care of the person I'd do anything for.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed the story doesn't hold together. Away on business with no word and no return date, that's not a trip. He left, packed a bag and walked out without saying when or whether he'd come back. I've been telling people the version that sounds less awful.
skillCheck.failText: He's traveling for work. She's managing fine.

templates.rewardFlavor: what I've been saving and a promise from both of us
templates.targetNpcOpener: I've been expecting this call for a few weeks now. Her sister sent word a month back that things were getting close. I've got my bag ready. Just tell me when to leave and I'll be on the road by morning.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell the sister to stop worrying so hard. She's doing better than she thinks.

templates.expositionText: My daughter can't walk anymore, and I've been carrying her everywhere for two months. I need a {quest_item} that might help her regain some strength. I've been told they exist, but I haven't been able to leave her long enough to find one.
templates.acceptText: Go quickly. She's patient but I'm not. Every day she loses a little more of what she used to be able to do, and I can see it in her face.
templates.declineText: I'll find one myself. I'll carry her with me if I have to.
templates.expositionTurnInText: She took it without complaint. She's always been braver than me. Her legs moved a little this evening, just a twitch, but I nearly shouted. There's one more thing keeping me from focusing entirely on her.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} outside have been getting close enough that I can hear them at night. She can't move on her own if something gets in. {kill_count} of them gone and I'll stop sleeping with one eye open.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I checked every window and every door twice and heard nothing. That silence is the most beautiful thing I've experienced in months.
templates.resolutionText: She stood up this morning, just for a moment, holding the chair, but she stood. I turned away so she wouldn't see my face. Take {reward_item}. You gave her a chance and me a reason to believe in more good days.

skillCheck.passText: You can tell the numbers don't work, can't you. If I'm getting by, why am I this desperate for help from a stranger. I sold my workshop tools last month to pay for remedies that didn't work. I have no trade left and no savings, and 'getting by' is the story I tell so people don't look at me with pity.
skillCheck.failText: We're getting by. That's the important thing.

templates.rewardFlavor: the last coins I have and a parent's deepest thanks

templates.expositionText: My younger brother lost his apprenticeship in {other_settlement} and came home with nothing. He won't talk about what happened, just sits and stares. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who trained him. I need to know what went wrong so I can help him find his footing again.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. He won't tell me anything, and I can't fix what I don't understand.
templates.declineText: I'll figure it out. Brothers always figure it out, eventually.
templates.expositionTurnInText: So that's what happened. I can see why he's ashamed, but It as bad as what I'd been imagining. Now I need to give him something to work toward.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a {quest_item} he's always talked about wanting. If I can put one in his hands, it might remind him that he's capable of the work, even if the last place didn't see it. Sometimes you need proof you can hold something before you believe you can build with it.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: He picked it up and turned it over in his hands for a long time. Then he looked at me and said thank you, which is the first full sentence he's spoken in two weeks.
templates.resolutionText: He's at the workbench this morning. Not talking yet, but working, which is his way of talking. Take {reward_item}. You helped me reach a person I couldn't reach alone, and that's a debt I'll carry gladly.

skillCheck.passText: All right, you're right to press me on this. I'm not just worried about his career. I'm worried he'll leave again, this time not for work but because he doesn't think he belongs anywhere. Our parents are gone.
skillCheck.failText: He's had a setback. People recover from setbacks.

templates.rewardFlavor: what I can pull together and a brother's gratitude
templates.targetNpcOpener: His brother sent someone? I'm not surprised he didn't come himself. The boy has talent, real talent, but he made a mistake that cost the shop a commission. I had to let him go.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell his brother the door isn't closed forever. Just closed for now. The boy needs to trust his hands again first.

templates.expositionText: My wife hasn't left the house in six weeks after something happened to her on the road. The {enemy_type_plural} prowling near the property make it worse, every sound from outside sends her further in. I need {kill_count} of them cleared so she can at least hear silence when she listens at the door.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. Silence is the first thing she needs, and I can't make it on my own.
templates.declineText: I'll keep the doors locked and the curtains drawn. It's what I've been doing.
templates.expositionTurnInText: She opened the curtain this afternoon. Just an inch, but the light came in and she didn't flinch. That's a step. I need to take the next one.
templates.conflict1Text: Find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role} who knew her before this started. I think hearing from someone outside these walls might remind her that the world isn't only the thing that scared her.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} sent a message back with you? She read it twice and put it under her pillow. That's the most she's engaged with anything from outside in weeks.
templates.conflict2Text: There's a {quest_item} she used to keep with her on every trip. I think putting it back in her hands might help her remember who she was before fear took over. She won't ask for it herself.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: She held it to her chest and closed her eyes. When she opened them, she looked at the door. Not with fear this time. More like someone considering a possibility.
templates.resolutionText: She walked to the gate this morning and stood there for five minutes before coming back inside. She smiled when she closed the door, and that was the first real smile in six weeks. Take {reward_item}. The road back is long, but she's on it now.

skillCheck.passText: You have a warmth about you that makes difficult things feel possible. I need that right now. The thing I haven't been able to tell anyone is that I'm angry. Not at her.
skillCheck.failText: She's recovering. I'm doing what I can for her.

templates.rewardFlavor: everything I can give and a family's lasting gratitude
templates.targetNpcOpener: She's not coming outside at all? That's not the woman I know. She used to walk further than anyone in {settlement_name} just for the pleasure of it. Whatever happened must have been terrible.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Remind her that the road is still there. It hasn't gone anywhere, and neither have the people on it who care about her.

templates.expositionText: My eldest took a bad blow to the head and the healer says rest and quiet, but there's a {quest_item} that could speed the mending. I've asked around {settlement_name} and nobody has one. If you can find one out there, I'll owe you more than I can calculate.
templates.acceptText: Please be fast. They keep saying they're fine, and they keep stumbling when they stand up.
templates.declineText: I'll look further. Maybe someone in {other_settlement} has heard of one.
templates.expositionTurnInText: They let me give it to them without arguing, which tells me more about how bad they're feeling than any complaint would. The color in their face is already better. But there's someone I need to involve.
templates.conflict1Text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who treated a similar injury years ago. I want them to hear what happened and tell me whether I'm doing enough. The doubt is eating at me.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} said the approach is right? I almost didn't believe you at first. I've been so convinced I was missing something that hearing I'm not feels strange.
templates.resolutionText: They ate a full meal today and complained about being bored. Complaining about boredom is the most hopeful thing I've heard in weeks. Take {reward_item}. You helped me believe I was doing the right things, and sometimes that's the help a parent needs most.

skillCheck.passText: You understand, don't you. In our family, there's a belief that injury to the head is injury to the spirit as well as the body. My grandmother taught me that healing requires attention to both, and I've been terrified that I'm only treating the part I can see. Your understanding tells me I'm not imagining the deeper concern.
skillCheck.failText: They took a hit and they need to heal. That's where things stand.

templates.rewardFlavor: a parent's savings and a gratitude too large for words
templates.targetNpcOpener: A head injury with dizziness and fatigue? I've seen this pattern before. The recovery is slow, but it's real, and the most important thing is patience. From what you're describing, the family is doing exactly what they should be.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell the parent to trust the process. Healing takes time, but it is happening.

templates.expositionText: There are {kill_count} {enemy_type_plural} closing on the only route out of here, and if I send the supplies that way, my brother won't have enough to last the week. But if I keep them, the people in {other_settlement} lose everything. I need those things cleared before I have to decide who goes without.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been standing here turning it over and over and getting nowhere. At least this buys me time.
templates.declineText: I understand. It your weight to carry. I just thought asking might make the choosing easier.
templates.expositionTurnInText: The road is open again, and that means the decision is real now. I've made my choice. The supplies go to {other_settlement}. But I need you to tell my brother why.
templates.conflict1Text: Find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role}, and they'll know how to reach my brother. Tell them what I've done and that I'm not asking forgiveness, just understanding. I couldn't look him in the eye and say it myself.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} said my brother already guessed what I'd choose. He told them he would have done the same. I don't know if that makes it better or worse.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I had a feeling this was coming. The family has been splitting itself in half over this for days. I'll get word to him. He won't be surprised, and he won't hold it against anyone.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them the message will reach him by morning. That's the best I can do.
templates.resolutionText: The supplies reached {other_settlement}, and my brother sent word that he's managing, which is the best either of us can say right now. Take {reward_item}. I owe you for carrying the message I couldn't.

skillCheck.passText: Honestly, the deciding part is already done, I made the call two days ago. The reason I haven't moved on it is that I keep hoping something will change so I don't have to live with being the one who chose. But nothing is going to change, is it.
skillCheck.failText: You're pushing pretty hard on this. It's a hard choice and I'll make it when the path is clear.

templates.rewardFlavor: the coin I'd set aside for the shipment and a family's quiet thanks

templates.expositionText: I need a {quest_item}. My daughter gave it away last season to buy peace with the traders from {other_settlement}, and I let her, because the alternative was worse. Now the deal has fallen through and I need it back before anyone realizes what we traded for nothing.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been sick over this since the day she handed it over. At least now there's a chance to undo it.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll find another way. She doesn't know yet that the deal collapsed, and I'd like to keep it that way a little longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You found it. Holding it again feels like borrowing time I already spent. But there's more trouble between here and putting things right.
templates.conflict1Text: {enemy_type_plural} have settled along the path my daughter takes to the market. {kill_count} of them dealt with and she can move freely again. I can't protect her from everything I've done, but I can protect her from that.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: The road is clear. She walked it this morning without knowing what you did, and that's exactly how I want it.
templates.resolutionText: I put the {quest_item} back where it belongs, and my daughter hasn't asked any questions. The deal is dead and we're no worse for it, thanks to you. Take {reward_item}. It's the least of what I owe.

skillCheck.passText: I mean, you know how these trade negotiations go, somebody has to bend. The thing is, she didn't give it away willingly. I told her it was the only option and framed the whole deal so she'd feel like she was choosing, but really I'd already decided for her. That's the part I can't sit with.
skillCheck.failText: You seem very interested in the details of how it happened. It was a family decision, and that's enough.

templates.rewardFlavor: what I kept hidden from the traders and a father's honest gratitude

templates.expositionText: I sent my son to work for {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} because we couldn't feed everyone through the winter. He's been there for months and I need to know if he's all right. {target_npc} is a {target_npc_role} who took him on as a favor to me, and I need to hear that the favor was worth what it cost.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I haven't slept properly since the day he left. Even knowing would be enough.
templates.declineText: I see. I'll keep waiting, then. I'm getting very good at waiting.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} says he's healthy and earning his keep. That's more than I let myself hope for. But there's something I need to send him, and I can't go myself.
templates.conflict1Text: Bring me a {quest_item}. It's small, but it belonged to his mother, and he'll know what it means. I want him to have something that tells him I didn't send him away because I stopped caring. I sent him away because I had no other answer.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: He'll have it by the end of the week. I keep imagining his face when he opens it, and I can't tell if it helps or makes it worse.
templates.targetNpcOpener: You're here about the boy? He's doing well, works hard, eats enough, doesn't complain. I can see he misses home, but he doesn't talk about it, got his mother's stubbornness about that.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them the boy is safe. That's the word that matters.
templates.resolutionText: He's safe and he has something of his mother's. That's the best I can do from here, and it will have to be enough. Take {reward_item}. You bridged a distance I've been too ashamed to cross myself.

skillCheck.passText: Fine. You want the truth that keeps me up at night? I had a choice between my son and my daughter, and I picked him because he's older and I thought he'd survive it better. I don't know if I was right, and I never will.
skillCheck.failText: Times were hard, and we did what we had to.

templates.rewardFlavor: the little I've saved since he left and a parent's lasting debt

templates.expositionText: The {enemy_type_plural} that have been circling {settlement_name} aren't going away, and every night they stay is a night closer to someone getting killed. I need {kill_count} of them put down. That part I can ask for plainly. The rest of what I need is harder to say.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'll explain the rest when you come back. I promise it will make sense.
templates.declineText: I understand. I wouldn't want this on my shoulders either, and I've had it on mine for weeks.
templates.expositionTurnInText: The perimeter is quiet again, and now for the part I've been dreading. My wife and I have been arguing about whether to send our youngest to stay with relatives in {other_settlement}. She wants to keep him close, but I think the danger here is too real.
templates.conflict1Text: Bring me a {quest_item}. Something I can pack for him that will make the journey bearable. If I'm going to do this, if I'm going to be the one who sends him away, I want him to have something good to hold onto.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's perfect. He'll like having something of his own for the road. Now I just need one more thing before I can let him go.
templates.conflict2Text: Go to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role}, and they've agreed to take him in. Tell them he's coming, and tell them he's scared even if he pretends he isn't. I want them to be ready for him.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} is expecting him. They said they'd make a bed and keep the lamp lit. I don't know why that detail is the one that broke me, but it did.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I've been waiting to hear. The boy is welcome here, and I'll treat him like my own until things settle down. I've got room, and I've got patience, which is what he'll need most.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Send him when he's ready. We'll be here.
templates.resolutionText: He left this morning. He waved from the road and I waved back and then I sat down in the doorway and didn't move for an hour. Take {reward_item}. You helped me do the thing I knew was right but couldn't face alone.

skillCheck.passText: You want me to stop circling it, fine. My wife told me if I send him away she won't forgive me, and I'm going to do it anyway because forgiveness won't mean much if he's dead. That's the choice I've made, and I'm making it with my eyes open.
skillCheck.failText: It's a family matter. We'll sort it out between ourselves.

templates.rewardFlavor: the savings I'd kept for his schooling and a father's word that it mattered

templates.expositionText: My mother asked me to find her a {quest_item}. She says it's for the house, but I know what it's really for. She's been putting her affairs in order for weeks, giving things away, tidying accounts. She's decided it's her time, and she's arranging things so the rest of us can carry on without her.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I know what I'm going to find when I come home one of these days, and I'd rather spend the time between now and then doing what she asks.
templates.declineText: It's all right. I'll find it myself. She's patient, and I have a little time left to use.
templates.expositionTurnInText: She smiled when I gave it to her. That calm, knowing smile she gets when things are going according to her plan. There's one more thing she asked for, and I think it's the last.
templates.conflict1Text: She wants to see {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} one more time. They're a {target_npc_role}, and they go back further than I do. Tell them she's asking and that she'd like it to be soon, but don't explain why. They'll understand.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} didn't ask questions. Just said they'd come. I think they already know, the same way I do.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I had a feeling I'd hear from her soon. We've known each other since before you were born, and there are some things old friends just sense. I'll come. Tell her I'll bring the good tea, the kind she always pretended not to like.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I'll be there within the week. She has my word.
templates.resolutionText: They sat together all afternoon and laughed about things I've never heard either of them mention before. She looked peaceful afterward, really peaceful, for the first time in months. Take {reward_item}. You gave her something no amount of coin could buy, and she wanted you to know it.

skillCheck.passText: You can see it, can't you. I keep saying the hardest part is letting her go, but that's not it. The hardest part is that she's choosing to leave, and she's right to, and I have to stand here and respect a decision that guts me. I'm not losing her. She's walking away with her eyes open, and I'm the one who can't look.
skillCheck.failText: She's old, and she's tired. That's as much as I want to say about it.

templates.rewardFlavor: a keepsake she set aside for whoever helped and my family's gratitude

templates.expositionText: The {enemy_type_plural} have been pressing closer, and I've made a decision I haven't told anyone yet. I'm giving up the homestead, letting it go so my family can resettle somewhere safer. But first I need {kill_count} of those things cleared so the road out is passable, because I'm not losing the house and my family on the same day.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. The decision is made. I just need the path clear to carry it out.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll find another way to get them out. I always do, somehow.
templates.expositionTurnInText: The road is open, and my wife doesn't know yet, but I'll tell her tonight. There's one more thing I need before we go.
templates.conflict1Text: Bring me a {quest_item}. There's something buried under the floorboards my grandfather left behind, and I need the right tool to get it out. I'm not leaving without it. Everything else can stay, but that comes with us.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I have it. I can feel the weight of four generations in my hand, and it's lighter than I thought it would be. We can leave now.
templates.resolutionText: We're going. The house will stand empty or it won't, and either way we'll be alive somewhere else. Take {reward_item}. It's from the life I'm leaving behind, and I'd rather it go to someone who helped than sit in a house nobody comes back to.

skillCheck.passText: You understand, then. The homestead isn't just a house, my family consecrated that ground when they first built here. Leaving means breaking a promise my grandmother made to this land. I've spent every night since the decision asking whether the ancestors will forgive me, and I still don't have an answer.
skillCheck.failText: It's just a house. A hard one to leave, but just a house.

templates.rewardFlavor: my grandfather's coin purse and the thanks of a family starting over

templates.expositionText: My husband passed three weeks ago, and the house still smells like his workshop. I found a list he left of things he meant to finish, and one of them was finding a {quest_item} for {settlement_npc}. I'd like to see it done. It won't bring him back, but it would mean one less promise he left broken.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I know it's a strange request. Finishing a dead man's errands. But it matters to me in a way I can't fully explain.
templates.declineText: I understand. It your burden. I just thought if someone else finished what he started, it might feel less like he left in the middle of something.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You found it. He would have been pleased. That's one thing crossed off his list, and it feels like a small door closing gently. There's one more thing I'd like to do, if you're willing.
templates.conflict1Text: My husband had a friend in {target_npc_settlement}, {target_npc}, a {target_npc_role}, and they don't know yet. I've been putting off sending word because saying it to someone who knew him makes it more real. Would you tell them? I can't do it in a letter.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} knows now. They sent back a message for me, and I'm not ready to read it yet, but I will be. Someday soon.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I wondered why he hadn't written. We'd been exchanging letters since before the frost, and when they stopped I told myself he was just busy. I'm glad someone came in person. He deserved better than a rumor reaching me months from now.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell her I'll come visit when the roads allow. She shouldn't sit in that house alone too long.
templates.resolutionText: His list is a little shorter, and his friend knows the truth. That's what I have to show for today, and it's more than I had yesterday. Take {reward_item}. He'd have wanted to pay properly for the help, and I'd like to honor that.

skillCheck.passText: It is nice having the list, you're right, gives you something to do in the morning. That's the part I don't say out loud. I've spent so many years thinking in pairs that I've forgotten how to want things on my own, and the errands keep me moving. I'm afraid of what happens when I run out of them.
skillCheck.failText: That's a kind thought, but I'd rather not go deeper into it. The errand is what matters.

templates.rewardFlavor: the payment he'd set aside for the job and my lasting thanks

templates.expositionText: My sister is buried past the ridge, and I haven't been able to visit since the {enemy_type_plural} moved in. I need {kill_count} of them cleared before I can get up there safely. It's been too long already, and the guilt of staying away is worse than the grief.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. She always said I worried too much. She'd probably say the same thing about this.
templates.declineText: I'll go eventually. When it's safe, or when I can't stand the waiting anymore. Whichever comes first.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I went up this morning. The stone is still standing, and someone, maybe the wind, had scattered wildflowers across the base. I sat there for an hour and didn't say anything. There's one more thing I'd like to bring her.
templates.conflict1Text: She used to keep a {quest_item} on her windowsill. Said it reminded her of the good parts. I'd like to leave one at the grave. Something that says I still remember the same things she did.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I set it next to the stone where the light hits in the afternoon. She would have liked that.
templates.resolutionText: I feel lighter. Not happier, exactly, but like something that was pressing on my chest has eased just enough to breathe around. Take {reward_item}. She would have insisted on paying for the help, and I'm not about to argue with her, even now.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed that the timing doesn't add up. You're right. The {enemy_type_plural} moved in three weeks ago, but I stopped visiting two months before that. I kept finding reasons, the weather, the path, my knees, and none of them were the real one. The real one is that I'm afraid I'll stand at her grave and realize I'm starting to forget her voice, and I don't want to be there when that happens.
skillCheck.failText: I just miss her. The rest is my own business.

templates.rewardFlavor: what she left me that I can bear to part with and a sibling's honest thanks

templates.expositionText: My boy died in the spring, and I still set two plates at supper without thinking. His teacher, {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}, doesn't know yet. They're a {target_npc_role} who cared about him, and I need someone to carry the news because I can't say it one more time.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've told so many people now that the words don't feel like words anymore. They feel like stones I keep having to pick up.
templates.declineText: I'll manage. I always manage. That's the cruelest part, how capable grief leaves you when it takes everything else.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} knows. I can feel it in the air, the way the world shifts a little when someone new carries the weight. There's something else, though. Something I've been putting off.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} that have been prowling near the house, they've been getting into his things. The shed where he kept his projects, his carvings, all of it. I need {kill_count} of them gone. I won't let them have the last pieces of him.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: The shed is untouched. His carvings are still on the shelf, exactly where he left them. I can close the door now and know they'll stay that way.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I could tell by the way you walked in. You don't bring that kind of quiet for good news. Tell me what happened. I'd rather hear it plainly than wonder.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I'll write to the family when I can find the right words. Give me a few days.
templates.resolutionText: The shed is safe, and his teacher knows. That's two things done, and neither of them will bring him back, but they let me set one plate at supper tonight and mean it. Take {reward_item}. He would have been embarrassed by the fuss, which is exactly why I'm making it.

skillCheck.passText: You want the part nobody asks about, fine. I'm angry, not at anyone in particular, just angry at the way it happened, angry at the season for turning, angry at the plate for being empty. Grief is supposed to be sad, and it is, but underneath that it's the angriest I've ever been in my life and I have nowhere to put it.
skillCheck.failText: I'm getting by. Some days are longer than others.

templates.rewardFlavor: the coins he'd saved in a jar by his bed and a parent's solemn thanks

templates.expositionText: My wife passed last month, and I've been going through her things slowly, one drawer at a time. She had a {quest_item} she'd been meaning to return to someone, and I'd like to see it found and sent back. She hated leaving things unfinished, and I hate it on her behalf.
templates.acceptText: I appreciate it. She kept everything organized. I'm just following the order she left behind.
templates.declineText: No need to explain. I'll get to it myself when I'm able. She was patient with me in life, and I expect she still is.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's the one. I can see her handwriting on the note pinned to it. She'd labeled everything, even the things she was giving back. The path to return it will take some figuring out, though.
templates.conflict1Text: There are {kill_count} {enemy_type_plural} between here and where this needs to go. I can't make the trip with those things out there, and I won't risk someone else going until the way is safe.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: The road is open again. Good. Now I can finish what she started.
templates.conflict2Text: Take it to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role}. She borrowed it years ago and kept meaning to bring it back. Tell them it comes with her apology for the delay, and that the apology is mine now.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} said they'd forgotten about it entirely, but they were glad to have it back. They asked about her, and I suppose you told them.
templates.targetNpcOpener: This is the piece she borrowed? I told her to keep it, but that was never her way, she always returned everything, even kindnesses. I'm sorry to hear she's gone. She was one of the careful ones.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell him I remember her fondly. That's the truth, and it's what he needs to hear.
templates.resolutionText: Her list is one item shorter, and her name was spoken kindly by someone who knew her. That's a good day, by the measure I use now. Take {reward_item}. She'd have paid you properly and probably fed you too, and I'm doing my best to be as generous as she was.

skillCheck.passText: All right, you pressed and I'll answer. The real reason I'm doing this isn't for her, it's because going through her things is the only way I can still spend time with her. Every drawer I open is another conversation, and when the last one is empty I don't know what I'll do with my hands.
skillCheck.failText: She left things in order, and I'm following it.

templates.rewardFlavor: what she'd set aside for exactly this purpose and a widower's quiet thanks

templates.expositionText: My grandmother used to sing while she worked, and when she died the house went quiet in a way I still haven't gotten used to. The {enemy_type_plural} around the property have made everything worse. I need {kill_count} of them gone so I can walk the grounds without jumping at every sound.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. The quiet is hard enough without adding fear to it.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll get used to both the silence and the noise eventually.
templates.expositionTurnInText: It's calmer now. I went out to her garden this morning and stood there without looking over my shoulder. That felt like progress. But there's something else I've been meaning to do.
templates.conflict1Text: She had a {quest_item} she kept on the mantle, said it reminded her of the village where she grew up. It broke during the last storm and I want to find a replacement. Not the same one, obviously, but something close enough that the mantle doesn't look so bare.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's close. Not the same, but she'd have understood the gesture. I put it where hers used to sit, and the room feels less empty now.
templates.resolutionText: The grounds are safe and the mantle has something on it again. Small things, both of them, but she lived in small things, and I think that's where I'll keep finding her. Take {reward_item}. She would have made you sit down for pie first, but I'm not the baker she was.

skillCheck.passText: You understand the weight of the small things, don't you. Everyone told me the big moments would be the hardest, the holidays, the anniversaries, but they were wrong. Every morning I come downstairs expecting to hear her humming, and every morning the kitchen is just a kitchen. That's the part that's killing me slowly.
skillCheck.failText: I miss her, and the house is quiet.

templates.rewardFlavor: her favorite coin purse and the thanks of a granddaughter who isn't done remembering

templates.expositionText: My brother asked me to do something before he died, and I promised I would. He wanted me to visit {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}, a {target_npc_role} they had unfinished business with. I don't know the details and I don't need to. I just need to keep my word.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I made the promise in his last hour, and I've been carrying it around like a stone ever since.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll find a way. A promise to a dying man doesn't expire because the road is long.
templates.expositionTurnInText: So {target_npc} and my brother had an old debt between them, and {target_npc} considers it settled now that word has been delivered. My brother would have been relieved. There's one more thing that would close this chapter.
templates.conflict1Text: Find me a {quest_item}. My brother wanted one placed at the crossroads where he and {target_npc} first met. Said it would mark the spot so neither of them would forget, even after. Especially after.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I placed it there myself, at the crossroads, where the path splits. The wind was blowing east, which was his favorite direction. I know that doesn't mean anything, but it felt right.
templates.targetNpcOpener: His brother sent you. I've been expecting this visit for longer than I'd like to admit. We had words between us, old ones, and he wanted them resolved before the end. Tell his brother the matter is closed, and that I wish I'd said so while there was still time to say it in person.
templates.targetNpcCloser: The debt is paid, both sides of it. Tell him I'm sorry it took this long.
templates.resolutionText: The promise is kept, the debt is settled, and there's a marker at the crossroads that will outlast both of us. Take {reward_item}. My brother left it with instructions, and you were the hands that carried it through.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed the mark on the stone he wanted placed. My brother believed the crossroads held a kind of memory, that places where paths split could hold the echo of the people who stood there and chose. I don't know if he was right, but I know he believed it completely, and I'm not going to let that belief go unhonored.
skillCheck.failText: It was important to him. That's reason enough for me.

templates.rewardFlavor: what my brother set aside for this exact purpose and a family's word kept

templates.expositionText: I've been telling people in {settlement_name} that I can handle more than they give me credit for, and I'm tired of hearing my own words without proof behind them. There are {kill_count} {enemy_type_plural} out there that everyone's been stepping around, and I want them dealt with. By me, through you, however it gets done. The point is it gets done and my name is on it.
templates.acceptText: Excellent. When this is over, nobody will be able to say I only talk. Let's move.
templates.declineText: Fine. I'll find another way to prove my point. I always do.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's the kind of result people notice. Word is already getting around that the {enemy_type_plural} are handled. Now I want to build on it before the momentum fades.
templates.conflict1Text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to finish a project I've been planning. Something visible, something that shows I can follow through on the big ideas, not just the small ones. Help me stockpile and I'll do the rest.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Look at this. I have everything I need to start building. This time next month, people will walk past and know who put it together.
templates.resolutionText: It's happening. The project is underway, the {enemy_type_plural} are handled, and {settlement_name} is looking at me differently. Take {reward_item}, and you'll have {reward_flavor} when the history gets written. I won't forget who was there at the beginning.

skillCheck.passText: Ha, yeah, everyone talks big around here, right? Honestly though, between you and me, I'm not as confident as I sound. I've failed at two things already this year and both times I smiled like it didn't sting. This one has to work because I'm running out of chances to be taken seriously, and that scares me more than the {enemy_type_plural} do.
skillCheck.failText: That's a funny question, I know what I'm doing. Let's leave it there.

templates.rewardFlavor: your name on the record

templates.expositionText: I want to expand, not just my shop but my whole operation. I've been running at capacity for months and turning away customers, and I refuse to keep doing that when the fix is sitting right in front of me. I need a {quest_item} to get the plans moving, something I can show to the people whose support I need.
templates.acceptText: Good. I've been waiting for someone who moves at my pace. This is going to be worth it for both of us.
templates.declineText: Your loss, honestly. This is going to happen with or without you. I'd just rather it happen faster.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Perfect. I can already see the pitch coming together. But plans on paper don't mean anything without backing from the right people.
templates.conflict1Text: Talk to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. They're a {target_npc_role} with connections I need. Tell them what I'm building and ask if they want in. If they say yes, that opens doors I can't open alone.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} said yes. Just like that. I knew they would if they heard it from someone besides me. Partners never sound credible pitching their own work.
templates.conflict2Text: Now the real build starts. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to begin the expansion properly. No shortcuts. I want this done right so nobody can say I cut corners when it matters.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That's everything I need to break ground. I can feel the shape of it already. This is going to change things in {settlement_name}.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Someone finally made the pitch. I've been hearing about this plan secondhand for weeks, and I was wondering when they'd get serious enough to send a proper messenger. Tell them I'm interested. The numbers make sense, and I like ambition that comes with a materials list.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I'll send my terms by the end of the week. This could work.
templates.resolutionText: The foundation is laid, the partners are signed, and I can finally stop talking about what I'm going to do and start showing people what I've done. Take {reward_item}. You weren't just running errands. You helped build something that's going to last, which is why you'll see {reward_flavor} when the sign goes up.

skillCheck.passText: You caught the part I'm not saying out loud. My father ran this same shop for thirty years and never once tried to make it bigger, and people told me I'd end up the same way, steady but small. Every board I lay is an answer to that prediction, and some mornings I'm not sure if I'm building something real or just building spite into a storefront.
skillCheck.failText: It's a good opportunity. I don't need a deeper reason than that.

templates.rewardFlavor: your name on the door

templates.expositionText: I've been working toward something for a long time, and I'm close enough now to taste it. But I need a favor from {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} before I can take the next step. They're a {target_npc_role}, and they owe me one from years back. Go remind them, politely, that it's time to pay up.
templates.acceptText: Wonderful. I've been patient long enough, and patience has done its job. Now it's time for action.
templates.declineText: Disappointing. But not surprising. Most people don't recognize momentum when they see it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} agreed? Good. That clears the political side of things. Now there's the practical matter standing between me and the finish line.
templates.conflict1Text: There are {kill_count} {enemy_type_plural} camped along the route I need for supply runs. Can't build an empire if the roads to it are blocked. Clear them out, and the last obstacle falls.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: The route is open, the backing is secured, and I'm standing where I've been imagining myself for years. It looks even better from here.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I had a feeling this day would come. They've been building toward something for years, and they've earned the right to call in what I owe. Consider the debt settled. I'll send word and the resources they need.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them it's done, and that I wish them well. They'll need it where they're headed.
templates.resolutionText: Everything is in motion. The favor is called, the road is clear, and I'm finally doing the thing I've been planning since I was old enough to plan. Take {reward_item}, and with it {reward_flavor} to whoever you want to carry it. You arrived at the exact right moment, and I believe in rewarding good timing.

skillCheck.passText: You want the real talk, fine. I've been told no so many times in my life that I stopped counting and started collecting, because every refusal is fuel. The people who laughed at this idea are going to watch it succeed, and I want them to feel exactly what I felt every time they dismissed me.
skillCheck.failText: I see an opportunity and I'm taking it. Simple as that.

templates.rewardFlavor: my personal endorsement

templates.expositionText: I've decided I'm going to be the best {self_role} this region has ever seen, not one of the best, the best. That starts with materials, and I need {gather_count} {quest_item} of the finest quality I can get. No excuses, no substitutions.
templates.acceptText: Now we're moving. I can already feel the shape of what this is going to become.
templates.declineText: I expected more, honestly. Not everyone can see what I'm building. That's fine. I'll remember who showed up and who didn't.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Real quality, exactly what I've been looking for. But materials alone don't make a masterwork. I need one more piece.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a {quest_item} that would make this project something people remember for years. It's rare, and it's out there somewhere, and I need someone willing to track it down. Bring it to me and watch what I do with it.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Do you see this? Do you see what we have? This is the piece that turns good into exceptional. I'm going to start tonight.
templates.resolutionText: It's done, and it's the finest thing I've ever made. People are already talking about it, and they're using my name in the same breath as the old masters. Take {reward_item}. And take {reward_flavor}, so you carry proof of what we pulled off together.

skillCheck.passText: You see the edge in me, and you're not wrong. There's a {self_role} in {other_settlement} who's been called the finest in the region for years, and their work is competent while mine is inspired. I've been watching them collect praise for mediocrity until it ate at me so badly I couldn't sit still anymore, and this piece is my answer.
skillCheck.failText: I take my craft seriously. That's all anyone needs to know.

templates.rewardFlavor: a signed piece, my best

templates.expositionText: I want to do something that outlasts me. Something {settlement_name} will still talk about in fifty years. That starts with clearing the {enemy_type_plural} that have been keeping everyone afraid to build beyond the walls. {kill_count} of them put down, and suddenly the land past the gate is ours again.
templates.acceptText: This is exactly the energy I needed. Let's make something people remember.
templates.declineText: Small thinking. I'll find someone with a longer view.
templates.expositionTurnInText: The perimeter is clear and people are already looking at the open land with new eyes. That's the beginning. Now I need someone with influence to see what I see.
templates.conflict1Text: Go to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}, a {target_npc_role} who's built the kind of thing I'm talking about. Ask them what it took, and ask them if they'll lend their name to what I'm starting. That kind of endorsement turns an idea into a movement.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} said yes. They actually said yes. I can see the whole thing unfolding now, every step of it, clear as morning.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I've heard the name mentioned a few times recently, and I was curious what they were after. Building something lasting for {settlement_name}? That takes nerve and materials in equal measure, and I respect anyone willing to put both on the table. I'll lend my support.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them they have my backing. Now let's see if they can deliver.
templates.resolutionText: The land is clear, the backing is real, and the first stones are going down this week. Take {reward_item}, and you'll have {reward_flavor} beside my own. Fifty years from now, someone will ask who helped build this, and your name will be in the answer.

skillCheck.passText: You see right through the big talk, don't you. I'm not building this because I love {settlement_name}. I'm terrified of being forgotten. Not dying, that's just mechanics, but passing through and leaving nothing behind. That fear has kept me awake more nights than I'll ever admit to anyone, and this project is the only answer I've found for it.
skillCheck.failText: I want to build something. That's reason enough.

templates.rewardFlavor: a place in the record

templates.expositionText: I've been passed over for leadership in {settlement_name} twice now, and both times they picked someone safer. I'm done being safe. I need a {quest_item} to present at the next council meeting, something that proves I've been doing the work nobody else was willing to do.
templates.acceptText: Perfect. When I walk into that room, I want them to see someone who solves problems, not someone who asks permission.
templates.declineText: I'll find another way. I didn't get this far by needing anyone's approval.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's the proof I needed. The council meets next week, and I'll have something in my hands instead of just words in my mouth. But I'm not going in half-prepared.
templates.conflict1Text: I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to fund the proposal I'm bringing. Not gifts, investments. I want the council to see that I've already started the work, that I'm not asking for the seat, I'm showing them I've already been sitting in it.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: The resources are secured. The proposal is real. Now I need one more piece to make this airtight.
templates.conflict2Text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who's been through this kind of push before. Ask them to speak on my behalf at the meeting, or at least to send a letter of support. Their word carries weight I haven't earned yet.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} agreed to write the letter. When they read that name at the council table, every head in the room will turn.
templates.targetNpcOpener: So they're making their move. I've been watching from a distance, and I think they've got the right instincts for it. The question was always whether they'd follow through, and sending you here tells me the answer. I'll write the letter.
templates.targetNpcCloser: It'll be in their hands before the meeting. Tell them to use it well.
templates.resolutionText: The meeting is set, the proposal is funded, and I have a letter from {target_npc} that will make them take me seriously for the first time. Take {reward_item}. Win or lose, {reward_flavor}, and that's a debt I pay in full when the day comes.

skillCheck.passText: You understand tradition, then, so you'll understand this. The council has always chosen its leaders from the same three families, and my family isn't one of them. That's not written anywhere but it's understood, and breaking that understanding scares them more than anything I might actually do. I'm breaking a rule nobody will admit exists.
skillCheck.failText: It's politics. Straightforward enough if you know the game.

templates.rewardFlavor: I remember who helped me

templates.expositionText: I found a second set of books hidden in {settlement_npc}'s desk. Different numbers, different names, and none of it matches what they've been showing me. I need a {quest_item} to compare the entries properly, something that will tell me whether the discrepancy is real or if I'm reading it wrong.
templates.acceptText: I appreciate it. I'd rather discover I'm wrong about this than discover I'm right.
templates.declineText: I'll figure it out myself. I always do, even when the answer is one I don't want.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I compared everything line by line, and the numbers don't lie, but apparently I do, to myself. The second ledger is a gift record, {settlement_npc} has been tracking charity donations they didn't want anyone to know about. I feel like a fool, but I still want to be sure.
templates.conflict1Text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who handles the other side of those donations. Go ask them about it, casually, without mentioning the ledger. If the story matches, I'll put this down and never bring it up again.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: It matches. Every entry. {settlement_npc} has been giving away coin to families who need it and keeping it secret because they didn't want the attention. I almost accused them of stealing, and they were doing the opposite.
templates.targetNpcOpener: You're asking about the donations? I wondered when someone would notice. Every month, regular as sunrise, a contribution arrives with no name attached, and I know exactly who it's from. Since you're asking directly, yes, it's genuine and it's been going on for over a year.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Don't make a fuss about it. They're doing it for the right reasons, and embarrassing them would defeat the purpose.
templates.resolutionText: I owe {settlement_npc} an apology I'll probably never give, because giving it would mean admitting I searched their desk. So instead I'll just be a better partner and stop looking for betrayal in every shadow. Take {reward_item}. You kept me from making an accusation that would have cost me the best person I know.

skillCheck.passText: You see the problem with what I just told you, don't you. I said I found discrepancies, but I went looking for them. I started with the conclusion and worked backward through the ledger until the numbers confirmed what I'd already decided. Every ambiguous entry fed the story I wanted to tell. I've done this before, and every time the answer turns out to be innocent, and every time I promise I'll stop.
skillCheck.failText: I just want the facts. That's all.

templates.rewardFlavor: a fair sum and my word that I'll try to see straight from now on

templates.expositionText: {settlement_npc} has been coming home late almost every night for two weeks, and the excuses change each time. I don't want to believe what I'm starting to believe, but the pattern is hard to ignore. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who lives near where {settlement_npc} claims to be spending the evenings. Go ask them what they've seen.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I know how this sounds. I know I might be wrong. But not knowing is worse than either answer.
templates.declineText: I understand. It's an ugly thing to ask someone to look into. I'll handle it myself.
templates.expositionTurnInText: So {target_npc} says {settlement_npc} has been helping repair a neighbor's roof every evening after work. The old woman there can't afford to hire anyone, and {settlement_npc} didn't want me to worry about the extra labor. The changing excuses were because they were embarrassed about how tired the work made them. I feel sick with relief.
templates.conflict1Text: I want to make this right without letting on what I suspected. Bring me a {quest_item}, something I can give {settlement_npc} as a gift. Something that says I appreciate them without explaining why. If I tell them what I was thinking, it'll hurt them, and they don't deserve that.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's perfect. Simple enough not to raise questions, generous enough to mean something. I'll give it to them tonight and let the whole ugly chapter close.
templates.targetNpcOpener: You're asking about {settlement_npc}? I see them most evenings, up on the widow Margel's roof patching holes before the rain season. Hard work, and they never mention it to anyone. I assumed the family knew.
templates.targetNpcCloser: There's nothing to worry about. They're just doing a kindness and being quiet about it.
templates.resolutionText: I gave {settlement_npc} the gift, and they looked at me like I'd said something they needed to hear without either of us speaking. Take {reward_item}. You saved me from myself, and that's not a debt I take lightly.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed the hole in what I'm asking you to do. I already have the answer. I checked the mud on their boots three days ago and it matched the clay near Margel's house, not the road to {other_settlement}. The excuses don't hold up and I can see that plainly. But knowing wasn't enough to stop the feeling, and the feeling has its own logic that ignores evidence entirely.
skillCheck.failText: I just need someone to confirm what I'm hoping is true.

templates.rewardFlavor: what I can put together quickly and my honest thanks for the discretion

templates.expositionText: {settlement_npc} has been keeping the back room locked, and when I asked about it they changed the subject so fast I could hear the conversation snap. I've never been shut out of any part of this house before. There's a {quest_item} I need to understand what's in there. Bring me one so I can make sense of what I'm seeing.
templates.acceptText: I appreciate it. I know it might be nothing. I know. But the lock is new and the silence around it is loud.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll sit with the not-knowing a while longer. I'm getting practiced at it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I compared what I found to what you brought me, and the pieces fit together in a way I didn't expect. The locked room is full of supplies for a surprise renovation of the kitchen. {settlement_npc} has been planning it for months and keeping it hidden because my birthday is next week. I've been losing sleep over a gift.
templates.conflict1Text: Now I need to fix what my suspicion nearly broke. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role}, and {settlement_npc} asked them to source some of the materials. I need you to go there and make sure my snooping hasn't ruined the surprise. If {target_npc} knows I was asking questions, the whole thing falls apart.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} doesn't suspect a thing. The secret is safe, the renovation will go ahead, and I'll act surprised on my birthday with a guilt that nobody will ever see on my face.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Looking for materials for the {settlement_name} order? Everything is on track, I've got the last pieces coming in by week's end. Tell them not to worry about the timeline, it'll be ready.
templates.targetNpcCloser: We're right on schedule. Nothing to report but good news.
templates.resolutionText: The kitchen will be beautiful, and {settlement_npc} will never know how close I came to ruining it with my own paranoia. Take {reward_item}. You helped me step back from the edge of something I would have regretted for years.

skillCheck.passText: You're right that I should just ask them directly, and the fact that I won't tells you something about me I'd rather you didn't know. I'm afraid of the conversation, not because of what they might say but because of what my asking would reveal about me. I don't trust the person I love most in the world, and the distrust comes from nowhere they caused, and I'd rather investigate in secret than face it in the open.
skillCheck.failText: I just need to understand what's going on. That's all.

templates.rewardFlavor: a fair payment and my promise to think before I doubt next time

templates.expositionText: A traveler from {other_settlement} has been spending a lot of time around {settlement_npc}, and every time I walk in they stop talking. I'm not the jealous type, except apparently I am, because I can't think about anything else. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who knows the traveler. Go ask them who this person is and what they're after.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I hate that I'm asking. I hate it. But the alternative is standing here imagining things that are probably worse than the truth.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll let it go. Or I'll try. That's the best I can promise right now.
templates.expositionTurnInText: So the traveler is {settlement_npc}'s cousin, visiting from the south, and they've been planning a family gathering that was meant to be a surprise. The whispering, the secrecy, all of it was for a celebration I was never supposed to know about. I've been torturing myself over a party invitation.
templates.conflict1Text: I need to do something to settle my conscience. Bring me a {quest_item}, something I can contribute to the gathering without letting on that I know. I want to show up with something generous and pretend the suspicion never happened. They deserve that much from me.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That'll be my contribution to the celebration. Anonymous, generous, and motivated entirely by guilt. Which is fine, because the result is the same.
templates.targetNpcOpener: You're asking about the visitor? That's family business, the good kind. The cousin has been planning a gathering for months, and they came to {settlement_name} to work out the details in person. It's meant to be a surprise for several people in the family, which is why all the secrecy.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell whoever sent you that there's nothing to worry about. It's a happy occasion.
templates.resolutionText: The gathering happened, and it was wonderful, and I sat there surrounded by people who love {settlement_npc} and felt ashamed of every dark thought I'd entertained. Take {reward_item}. You stopped me from saying something at exactly the wrong moment, and I owe you for that silence.

skillCheck.passText: Fine, you want the unvarnished version. I wasn't just suspicious, I was rehearsing the confrontation with the words lined up, the accusations organized, the whole performance ready to go. If you hadn't agreed to check first I would have walked in there tonight and blown apart my marriage over a family reunion. That's how close it was.
skillCheck.failText: I'm probably overreacting. I just want to be sure.

templates.rewardFlavor: a fair sum and the deepest thanks of someone who nearly ruined their own happiness

templates.expositionText: There's a new {settlement_npc_role} in {settlement_name} who does the same work I do, and everyone has been going to them instead of me. I found out they've been sourcing a {quest_item} I can't get, and I want to know where it comes from. Bring me one so I can see what I'm competing against.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I know it sounds petty. But watching your livelihood walk out the door smiling is a particular kind of pain.
templates.declineText: Fine. I'll keep watching the traffic go past my door and pretending it doesn't bother me.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I've looked at it carefully, and it's good work, but It the miracle I'd built it up to be in my head. The truth is they're doing the same quality I do, just with a fresher face and more enthusiasm. I stopped believing in my own work somewhere along the way, and I want to hear it from someone outside {settlement_name}.
templates.conflict1Text: Talk to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}, a {target_npc_role} who's seen both our work. I want an honest assessment from someone who has no reason to spare my feelings. If they say I've fallen behind I'll accept it, and if they say I haven't I'll stop punishing myself.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} said my work is excellent and always has been, and that losing customers to a newcomer is normal and temporary. They also said the new person has been sending people my way for jobs they can't handle. I didn't know that. I didn't know any of that.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I know both operations, and I'll tell you straight. The work from {settlement_name} has always been reliable, and that hasn't changed. Newcomers draw attention because they're new, and it always settles down. Between you and me, the new one has been referring overflow work back to the original shop for weeks.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them to keep doing what they're doing. The quality speaks for itself.
templates.resolutionText: I walked over to the new {settlement_npc_role}'s shop this morning and introduced myself properly. We talked for an hour, and by the end of it I felt foolish for every unkind thought I'd had. Take {reward_item}. You showed me that the competition I was afraid of was entirely in my own head.

skillCheck.passText: You have a way of making people drop their guard, so all right. I was good once, really good, and somewhere in the last few years I stopped trying to be excellent and started just being adequate. I let my customers go by being too comfortable to fight for them, and that's what the jealousy is really about.
skillCheck.failText: It's a business matter. I'll sort it out.

templates.rewardFlavor: a sample of my best recent work and fair coin for your time

templates.expositionText: I found a letter in {settlement_npc}'s coat, unsigned, written in a hand I don't recognize, and it reads like a love letter. My stomach hasn't unclenched since I opened it. I need a {quest_item} to compare the ink and paper. I know that sounds desperate, but I need to do something with my hands besides shaking.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I keep rereading the letter looking for something I missed, and every time I find a new way to make it worse.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll sit with it. Maybe by morning the words will have rearranged themselves into something I can live with.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I compared everything, the ink, the paper, the way the letters sit on the page, and it matches the stationery from the old schoolhouse. The handwriting belongs to {settlement_npc}'s mother, who writes to them every season and never signs because she says family shouldn't need signatures. It's a letter from a mother to a child, and I read it as a betrayal.
templates.conflict1Text: I need to do something right after doing so much wrong in my own head. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} is a {target_npc_role} who carries mail from the old schoolhouse district. Ask them to confirm the handwriting so I can put this to rest completely and stop torturing myself with a dead woman's penmanship.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} confirmed it immediately. Said they'd recognize that handwriting anywhere, and that she writes the same way to all her children. I feel smaller than I've felt in years, and I earned every inch of it.
templates.targetNpcOpener: That handwriting? I'd know it blind, she writes to all her children the same way, long, warm, and never signed. Says putting a name at the bottom makes it feel like business instead of love. I've carried those letters for years.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Whoever is worried can stop. It's just a mother's love on paper.
templates.resolutionText: I put the letter back in the coat pocket and I'll never mention that I read it. {settlement_npc} deserves their mother's words without my fingerprints on them. Take {reward_item}. You helped me see the shape of my own worst instinct before it did any real damage.

skillCheck.passText: You noticed the pressed flower in the envelope. I noticed it too and thought it was a token of something romantic, but you're right that it's silverveil, and it only grows near old family plots. She sends them pressed into every letter because it connects the words to the family ground. I was so busy looking for a betrayal that I missed a blessing sitting right in front of me.
skillCheck.failText: I just need to know who wrote it. That's all.

templates.rewardFlavor: what coin I can spare and the thanks of someone learning to trust better

templates.expositionText: I reported {settlement_npc} to the trade council for watering down supplies, and they were stripped of their stall. I was so certain I was right. But I've been finding signs that the bad supplies came from a completely different source, and now someone's livelihood is ruined because I didn't check. I need a {quest_item} to compare against the original evidence.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. Every day that passes makes this harder to undo.
templates.declineText: I understand. It's my mess, and I shouldn't be surprised if nobody wants to wade into it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Holding this against the samples I kept, it's obvious. The bad goods are a different grade entirely. {settlement_npc} was selling clean stock and I accused them of something they never did. I need a witness who can confirm it.
templates.conflict1Text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} handled the original inspection when the complaint went through. They'll remember what they saw. Ask them to look at the evidence again, because I think the council made its decision on my anger, not on facts.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: So {target_npc} agrees the samples don't match. The council acted on my word alone and nobody questioned it. That's what happens when someone speaks with enough certainty. People stop asking whether they should believe it.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I remember that inspection. Frankly, I had doubts at the time, but the complaint was so forceful that the council didn't want to slow things down. Looking at the evidence now with fresh eyes, the contamination doesn't match what that stall was selling. Someone else's stock got mixed in before it reached the buyers.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I'll file a corrected report. The stall should be reinstated, and sooner rather than later.
templates.resolutionText: The council reversed the ruling this morning, and {settlement_npc} gets their stall back. I told them to their face what I'd done, and they didn't forgive me. Take {reward_item}. You helped me stop a wrong from becoming permanent.

skillCheck.passText: Well, since you've heard what happened at the council, I might as well be straight with you. I knew the evidence was thin when I filed the complaint. I didn't like {settlement_npc} personally, and I let that feeling dress itself up as righteousness. The council trusted my word and I spent that trust on a grudge.
skillCheck.failText: That's an odd angle to come at this from. I made a mistake, and that's the whole story.

templates.rewardFlavor: what I owe and then some, because the debt is bigger than coin

templates.expositionText: My neighbor's child was caught near the storehouse the same night supplies went missing, and the whole settlement decided that was enough proof. I didn't defend the kid, even though I've known that family for years, and something about this doesn't sit right with me anymore. Would you talk to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}? They trade with the storehouse regularly and might know who else had reason to be there that night.
templates.acceptText: Good. That child has been walking around like a ghost since the accusation, and their family can barely hold their heads up.
templates.declineText: I suppose It your problem. But someone should be asking these questions, and nobody here will.
templates.expositionTurnInText: So {target_npc} says a trader from outside passed through that evening with a short cart and left with a full one. Nobody stopped them, nobody asked a question. Meanwhile a child who happened to be playing outside gets blamed for the whole thing.
templates.conflict1Text: I want to bring proof to the elders that puts this to rest. The trader would have needed a {quest_item} to move that much stock quietly. Find me one so I can show the council exactly how the theft was done, and by whom.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That matches perfectly. The cart tracks, the timing, the {quest_item}. Anyone looking at this honestly will see the child had nothing to do with it. I should have spoken up sooner.
templates.targetNpcOpener: I saw that trader the night everything went missing. Came in with almost nothing on the cart and left heavy. I didn't think much of it at the time, figured it was legitimate business. But now that you mention missing supplies, the timing lines up too well to ignore.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I'd swear to it in front of anyone. That trader was the one moving goods out, not some child.
templates.resolutionText: The elders reviewed everything and cleared the child publicly. Their mother cried, and I nearly did too, because I should have fought harder from the start. Take {reward_item}. You did what I was too slow to do.

skillCheck.passText: You trade with the storehouse too? Then you know how things work over there, the security, who comes and goes. The reason I stayed quiet is that the storehouse manager is a friend of mine and I didn't want to suggest his security failed. I let a child take the fall to protect a grown man's reputation.
skillCheck.failText: You're asking questions that sound friendly but feel pointed. I said I should have spoken up, and that's all I've got.

templates.rewardFlavor: a family's gratitude and everything I can put together

templates.expositionText: A traveler passed through {settlement_name} last week and was accused of poisoning our well. We chased them out without hearing a word they had to say. Now I've found the real cause, rot in the wooden casings, and I can't live with what we did. I need a {quest_item} to repair the well properly so there's no question about what happened.
templates.acceptText: I appreciate it. The traveler is probably halfway to the coast by now, but the least I can do is fix what's actually broken.
templates.declineText: I see. I suppose fixing a well isn't the most exciting work. But someone innocent got driven out for nothing, and that bothers me more than the water.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Good. With this I can show anyone who asks that the contamination was structural, not deliberate. But I still need to find the traveler, or at least send word that they were wrongly accused.
templates.conflict1Text: {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} takes in travelers passing through on the south road. If the person we chased out went that direction, {target_npc} would know. Go and ask if they've seen anyone matching that description, and if they have, tell them we know the truth now.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: So {target_npc} found them, and they're safe but shaken. They told {target_npc} they'd never set foot in {settlement_name} again, and honestly, I can't argue with that. At least they know the accusation has been dropped.
templates.targetNpcOpener: That traveler came through here two days ago, still upset. They said a whole settlement turned on them over a poisoned well and nobody would listen. I gave them a bed and a meal. They're a decent person who got treated like a criminal for no reason.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I'll pass along the message. Whether they accept it is up to them.
templates.resolutionText: The well is repaired, the word is out, and a few people in {settlement_name} are finally willing to admit we were wrong. Not all of them, but enough. Take {reward_item}. You helped me make right what I could, even if I can't undo all of it.
